condition_specific: Primary brain cancer, malignant melanoma, leukemia, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, bladder cancer, ureter cancer, kidney cancer, prostate cancer, breast cancer
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Firefighter must demonstrate exposure to a known IARC or NTP carcinogen associated with the disabling cancer. Annual medical exam required for each of first 7 years. Fire Fighter I certification required. Presumption covers paid and registered volunteer firefighters.

condition_specific: Primary brain cancer, malignant melanoma, leukemia, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, bladder cancer, ureter cancer, kidney cancer, prostate cancer, breast cancer
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Volunteer firefighters registered with state fire marshal or for WC. Same terms as career firefighters. Carcinogen exposure demonstration required.

condition_specific: Cardiovascular events experienced within 72 hours after exposure to smoke, fumes, or toxic substances
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Event must occur within 72 hours after exposure to smoke, fumes, or toxic substances. Tobacco exclusion applies. Annual medical exam required for each of first 7 years. Fire Fighter I certification required. Presumption rebutted by preponderance.

condition_specific: Cardiovascular events experienced within 72 hours after exposure to smoke, fumes, or toxic substances
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Volunteer firefighters registered with state fire marshal or for WC. Same terms as career firefighters.

condition_specific: Respiratory disease
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Tobacco exclusion: does not apply to firefighters with history of tobacco use for cardiovascular or lung conditions. Annual medical exam required for each of first 7 years. Firefighter must hold at minimum Fire Fighter I certification. Presumption may be rebutted by preponderance including tobacco use, fitness, lifestyle, hereditary factors, and other exposures. Covers firefighters under AS 23.30.243 (WC-covered fire fighters).

condition_specific: Respiratory disease
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: 'Fire fighter' defined to include members of volunteer fire departments registered with state fire marshal or registered for WC purposes. Same terms and conditions as career firefighters.

condition_specific: Cancer caused by exposure to a known carcinogen reasonably linked to the disabling cancer
notes: Must manifest during service AND firefighter must demonstrate exposure to known IARC carcinogen. Rebuttable by preponderance. Death from cancer within 10 years of last employment is treated as line-of-duty death (§11-43-144(c)(2)). Post-termination window of 10 years applies to death benefit only. Effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: Hypertension, heart disease
notes: Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence. Applies to city/municipal firefighters (§11-43-144) and state-employed firefighters (§36-30-40/41). Both sections use identical occupational disease list. Pre-employment exam required unless hired before Sept 8, 1967 (municipal) or before Jan 1, 2013 (state). Effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: Respiratory disease
notes: Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence. Applies to city/municipal firefighters (§11-43-144) and state-employed firefighters (§36-30-40/41). Effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: HIV
notes: Must manifest during employment AND firefighter must demonstrate line-of-scope exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: Hepatitis
notes: Must manifest during service AND firefighter must demonstrate line-of-scope exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: Parkinson's disease caused by exposure to a known toxin associated with increased risk
notes: Must manifest during service AND firefighter must demonstrate exposure to known toxin associated with Parkinson's risk. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: Leukemia, lymphoma, mesothelioma, multiple myeloma; cancer of brain, digestive tract, urinary tract, liver, skin, breast, cervix, thyroid, prostate, testicle; cancer with higher firefighter incidence linked to known carcinogen
notes: Three statutory mechanisms: (1) § 21-5-705 provides death benefit up to $150,000 for firefighters killed in line of duty from covered cancers after Jan 1, 2012 — firefighter must be under 68; Firefighter Benefit Review Panel makes recommendations to Arkansas State Claims Commission. (2) § 24-4-511 (APERS) and § 24-10-607 (LOPFI) provide disability retirement presumption for cancer diagnosed 'while in the line of duty.' Both rebuttable by preponderance. § 24-4-511 requires 5 years actual service including 18 of last 24 months; members hired after July 1, 2013 under § 24-10-607 require 10 years. Firefighter must not have been diagnosed with cancer prior to service start. LOPFI section also covers volunteer members.

condition_specific: Leukemia, lymphoma, mesothelioma, multiple myeloma; cancer of brain, urinary tract, liver, skin, breast, cervix, thyroid, prostate, testicle, colon, digestive tract; cancer with higher firefighter incidence linked to known carcinogen
notes: Both APERS (§ 24-4-511) and LOPFI (§ 24-10-607) explicitly extend cancer presumption to 'volunteer firefighter' / 'volunteer member.' Rebuttable by preponderance. Cancer must be diagnosed 'while in the line of duty.'

condition_specific: Brain cancer, bladder cancer, rectal cancer, colon cancer, lymphoma, leukemia, adenocarcinoma, mesothelioma of the respiratory tract
notes: Applies to firefighters and peace officers with identical terms for this cancer list. Must be assigned to hazardous duty for at least 5 years. Must have been exposed to known IARC carcinogen and informed department of exposure. Post-termination: applies to former firefighters/peace officers age 65 or younger diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion applies to respiratory tract cancers only if tobacco is a substantial contributing cause. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence showing specific non-occupational cause.

condition_specific: Buccal cavity and pharynx cancer, esophageal cancer, large intestine cancer, lung cancer, kidney cancer, prostate cancer, skin cancer, stomach cancer, testicular cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, multiple myeloma, malignant melanoma
notes: Firefighter-only expanded cancer list (not available to peace officers). Additional requirement: NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam. Must have been exposed to known IARC carcinogen. Former firefighters age 65 or younger diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion for respiratory tract cancers where tobacco is substantial contributing cause.

condition_specific: Brain cancer, bladder cancer, rectal cancer, colon cancer, lymphoma, leukemia, adenocarcinoma, mesothelioma of the respiratory tract
notes: 'Peace officer' means full-time peace officer regularly assigned to hazardous duty as part of special operations, SWAT, EOD, or hazmat response unit. Must be 65 or younger and diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion for respiratory tract cancers. Rebuttable by preponderance.

condition_specific: Heart-related, perivascular, or pulmonary injury, illness, or death
notes: Event must occur within 24 hours after a known exposure. NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam required. Covers firefighters and volunteer firefighters under § 23-901(6)(d). Tobacco exclusion applies where tobacco is a substantial contributing cause. Rebuttable by preponderance showing specific non-occupational cause.

condition_specific: Heart-related, perivascular, or pulmonary injury, illness, or death
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 23-901(6)(d) definition. Event must occur within 24 hours after known exposure. NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam required.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD); workers' compensation coverage for PTSD with accepted WC claim
statute_citation: Ariz. Rev. Stat. § 23-972 (conditional on FDA approval of midomafetamine)
notes: Employers shall provide WC coverage for firefighters diagnosed with PTSD by a licensed mental health professional with an accepted WC claim. This is a coverage mandate, not a traditional rebuttable presumption. Section 23-972 conditionally enacted only if FDA approves midomafetamine (MDMA) by December 31, 2025 — FDA did not approve as of the 2026 data date; status uncertain. Separate from the counseling program under § 38-673 (which expressly does not create a WC presumption).

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD); workers' compensation coverage for PTSD with accepted WC claim
statute_citation: Ariz. Rev. Stat. § 23-972 (conditional on FDA approval of midomafetamine)
notes: Covers 'certified peace officers' (§ 38-842). Same conditional enactment tied to FDA approval of midomafetamine by December 31, 2025. Coverage mandate with accepted WC claim, not a traditional shifting presumption.

condition_specific: Cancer including leukemia caused by exposure to known IARC carcinogen
statute_citation: Cal. Lab. Code § 3212.1 (William Dallas Jones Cancer Presumption Act of 2010)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 120 months
notes: Covers active firefighting members (volunteer, partly paid, fully paid) of city/county/district/state fire departments, UC/CSU, Cal Fire, county forestry; also peace officers (§830.1, §830.2(a), §830.37(a)(b)) primarily in active law enforcement; fire and rescue services coordinators at Cal OES. Member must demonstrate exposure to known IARC carcinogen. Rebuttable if carcinogen not reasonably linked to disabling cancer and primary site established. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Post-termination max is 120 months (10 years) — longer than most other CA presumptions.

condition_specific: Cancer including leukemia caused by exposure to known IARC carcinogen
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 120 months
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly included in § 3212.1(a)(1) definition of covered active firefighting members.

condition_specific: Cancer including leukemia caused by exposure to known IARC carcinogen
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 120 months
notes: Peace officers defined in § 830.1, § 830.2(a), § 830.37(a)(b) primarily engaged in active law enforcement. Also covered under Government Code § 31720.6 (safety members with 5+ years service, county pension systems).

condition_specific: Heart trouble, pneumonia
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers members of city/county/district fire departments (volunteer, partly paid, fully paid), Department of Forestry and Fire Protection active firefighting members, county forestry/firefighting departments, and warden service of Wildlife Protection Branch. Also covers sheriff's offices, California Highway Patrol, DA investigator staff, and police departments for hernia (not mapped here). Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Disease must develop or manifest during service.

condition_specific: Heart trouble (disability retirement basis)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: County pension system (Chapter 4 / Chapter 5 systems). Requires 5 years service. Covers safety members, firefighter members (including active fire suppression members not classified as safety members), and members in active law enforcement. Disease not attributed to pre-existing condition. Rebuttable.

condition_specific: Heart trouble, pneumonia
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers members of sheriff's offices, police departments, California Highway Patrol, DA investigator staff, and warden service of Wildlife Protection Branch. Heart trouble and pneumonia only (hernia also included for these groups but is 'other' category). Must develop or manifest during service.

condition_specific: Heart trouble (disability retirement basis)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: County pension system. 5 years service required. Covers members in active law enforcement.

condition_specific: Blood-borne infectious disease; MRSA skin infection
post_termination_formula: Blood-borne: 3 months per year, max 60 months; MRSA: flat 90 days
notes: Covers sheriff's offices, police/fire departments, Cal Fire active firefighting members, county forestry, Penal Code Chapter 4.5 individuals. Blood-borne infectious disease: post-termination window is 3 months/year, max 60 months. MRSA skin infection: separate flat 90-day post-termination window. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026 (§ SEC. 5 / § 3212.8(e)).

condition_specific: Tuberculosis
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers regular fully-paid fire department members plus active Cal Fire firefighting members. Employer may require pre-employment TB test. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Government Code § 31720.92 provides parallel disability retirement presumption (county pension systems).

condition_specific: Meningitis
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers regular full-time salaried fire department members and county forestry/firefighting. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Government Code § 31720.93 provides parallel disability retirement presumption.

condition_specific: COVID-19
statute_citation: Cal. Gov. Code § 7523 (COVID-19 Disability Retirement Presumption)
notes: Expired: § 7523.2 repealed this article effective January 1, 2024. Covered public retirement system members in firefighter and peace officer job classifications under § 3212.87. Rebuttable presumption of service-related disability for COVID-19-related disability retirement.

condition_specific: Blood-borne infectious disease; MRSA skin infection
post_termination_formula: Blood-borne: 3 months per year, max 60 months; MRSA: flat 90 days
notes: Volunteer fire department members covered ('whether those persons are volunteer, partly paid, or fully paid').

condition_specific: Blood-borne infectious disease; MRSA skin infection
post_termination_formula: Blood-borne: 3 months per year, max 60 months; MRSA: flat 90 days
notes: Covers sheriff's offices, police departments, Penal Code Chapter 4.5 individuals. Also covered under Government Code § 31720.7 (county pension system members including probation officers).

condition_specific: Tuberculosis
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers full-time salaried police, sheriff, CHP, DA investigators, and prison/jail guards and correctional officers employed by a public agency. Government Code § 31720.92 provides parallel disability retirement presumption (county pension systems).

condition_specific: Meningitis
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers regular full-time salaried police, sheriff, CHP, county probation officers, DA investigators. Government Code § 31720.93 provides parallel disability retirement presumption.

condition_specific: COVID-19
statute_citation: Cal. Gov. Code § 7523 (COVID-19 Disability Retirement Presumption)
notes: Expired: § 7523.2 repealed this article effective January 1, 2024. Covered public retirement system members in peace officer job classifications under § 3212.87.

condition_specific: Tuberculosis
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Prison or jail guards and correctional officers employed by a public agency are explicitly covered under § 3212.6.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress or mental health disorder (PTSD or mental health condition diagnosed under this division)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Sunset provision: section repealed January 1, 2029 (per § 3212.15(g)). Applies to injuries occurring on or after January 1, 2020. Covers same firefighter population as § 3212.1 plus peace officers (§ 830.1, §830.2(a)(b)(c), §830.32, §830.37(a)(b), §830.5, §830.55) and Cal OES fire/rescue coordinators. Government Code § 31720.91 provides parallel disability retirement presumption (county pension systems) — repealed January 1, 2032 per SB 623 extension.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress or mental health disorder (PTSD or mental health condition diagnosed under this division)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly included (active firefighting members, 'whether volunteers, partly paid, or fully paid'). Sunset: repealed January 1, 2029.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress or mental health disorder (PTSD or mental health condition diagnosed under this division)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Peace officers defined in Penal Code §§ 830.1, 830.2(a)(b)(c), 830.32, 830.37(a)(b), 830.5, 830.55 primarily in active law enforcement. Sunset: repealed January 1, 2029.

condition_specific: Hernia
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Hernia must develop or manifest during service. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026.

condition_specific: Illness or death due to exposure to biochemical substance (chemical warfare agent, weaponized biological agent, nuclear or radiological agent)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers peace officers (§§ 830.1-830.5) and members of fire departments including volunteers, partly paid, fully paid, Cal Fire, county forestry. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Government Code § 31720.9 provides parallel disability retirement presumption.

condition_specific: Illness or death due to exposure to biochemical substance (chemical warfare agent, weaponized biological agent, nuclear or radiological agent)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Volunteers explicitly included in 'members of a fire department' definition under § 3212.85(e)(2).

condition_specific: Hernia
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Covers sheriff's offices, police, CHP, DA investigators. Regular salaried county/city-county peace officers also covered for hernia manifesting during service.

condition_specific: Illness or death due to exposure to biochemical substance (chemical warfare agent, weaponized biological agent, nuclear or radiological agent)
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: Peace officers under Penal Code §§ 830.1-830.5. Government Code § 31720.9 provides parallel disability retirement presumption for county pension system peace officer and firefighter members.

condition_specific: Lower back impairment
post_termination_formula: 3 calendar months per full year of requisite service, max 60 months
notes: County pension system disability retirement presumption. Member must have been employed full-time for at least 5 consecutive months in a calendar year in the listed classification AND required to wear a duty belt as a condition of employment.

condition_specific: Brain, skin, digestive system, hematological system, genitourinary system cancers
notes: Applies to full-time firefighters employed minimum 1,600 hours annually. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal substantial evidence of the condition. Employer may rebut by preponderance of medical evidence.

condition_specific: Coronary thrombosis, cerebral vascular accident, myocardial infarction, cardiac arrest (excludes hypertension and angina)
notes: Requires 5 years continuous full-time employment. Malfunction must occur during or within 48 hours after a qualifying work event (fire suppression, rescue, hazmat, EMS, disaster relief, or other emergency response). Benefits reduced 25% if firefighter smoked tobacco within 5 years preceding event. Benefits offset by WC, pension, Social Security, retirement, and other employer-paid income. Maximum $250,000 per event. This is a benefit statute under Title 29, not a WC presumption under Title 8.

condition_specific: Coronary thrombosis, cerebral vascular accident, myocardial infarction, cardiac arrest (excludes hypertension and angina)
notes: Employer may (not shall) provide equivalent benefits for volunteer firefighters. Requires 5 years continuous service with the same employer. Malfunction must occur during or within 48 hours after a qualifying work event. Benefits reduced 25% if firefighter smoked tobacco within 5 years preceding event.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure within 2 days of discovery; employer must provide baseline test within 5 days of reported exposure; diagnosis must occur within 24 months of exposure
notes: Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that exposure did not occur on the job.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure within 2 days of discovery; employer must provide baseline test within 5 days of reported exposure; diagnosis must occur within 24 months of exposure
notes: Statute covers 'emergency services providers.' Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure within 2 days of discovery; employer must provide baseline test within 5 days of reported exposure; diagnosis must occur within 24 months of exposure
notes: Statute covers 'peace officers.' Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Conditions affecting skin, brain, skeletal, digestive, endocrine, respiratory, lymphatic, reproductive, urinary, or hematological systems
filing_deadline_notes: Surviving dependents may file within 1 year of firefighter's death
notes: Requires 5 years as interior structural firefighter or equivalent fire marshal/investigator. Tobacco exclusion: no cigarette use during 15-year period prior to diagnosis. Benefits offset by other benefits received. If firefighters cancer relief account becomes insolvent, municipal/state employers no longer obligated to provide compensation. Effective October 1, 2023.

condition_specific: Hypertension, heart disease
notes: Applies ONLY to uniformed paid municipal firefighters who began employment BEFORE July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of hypertension or heart disease. Benefits provided in lieu of chapter 568 WC benefits for hypertension/heart disease. Includes retirement/survivor benefits. 'Municipal employer' as defined in § 7-467.

condition_specific: Cardiac arrest or myocardial infarction occurring within 24 hours after duty completion
filing_deadline_notes: Employer must contest liability on or before the 28th day after receiving written notice of claim or presumption of compensability is conclusive
notes: Applies to uniformed paid municipal firefighters hired after July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam by licensed physician must have failed to reveal evidence of condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Hypertension, heart disease
notes: Applies ONLY to regular paid municipal police officers who began employment BEFORE July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of hypertension or heart disease. Benefits provided in lieu of chapter 568 WC benefits. Includes retirement/survivor benefits.

condition_specific: Cardiac arrest or myocardial infarction occurring within 24 hours after duty completion
filing_deadline_notes: Employer must contest liability on or before the 28th day after receiving written notice of claim or presumption of compensability is conclusive
notes: Applies to regular members of paid municipal police departments hired after July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam by licensed physician must have failed to reveal evidence of condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers uniformed paid and volunteer firefighters. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers uniformed members of paid and volunteer fire departments. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers members of volunteer ambulance services. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers regular paid municipal police officers and constables. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure. Medical records subject to workers' compensation guidelines.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Covers emergency medical services personnel. Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Covers Department of Correction employees and telecommunicators. Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.

condition_specific: Leukemia; breast, ovarian, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, colon, colorectal, liver, testicular, or respiratory cancer; multiple myeloma; brain, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, throat, kidney, thyroid, or bladder cancer following toxic substance exposure
notes: Covers sworn firefighters. Requires 10 years of service. Second cancer category (brain, non-Hodgkin's, throat, kidney, thyroid, bladder) requires documented toxic substance exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Leukemia; breast, ovarian, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, colon, colorectal, liver, testicular, or respiratory cancer; multiple myeloma; brain, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, throat, kidney, thyroid, or bladder cancer following toxic substance exposure
notes: Covers non-sworn EMS employees. Requires 10 years of service. Second cancer category requires documented toxic substance exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Heart disease, hypertension
notes: Covers sworn firefighters with DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Pre-employment exam must have shown freedom from condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence to the contrary. Coverage denied if eligible vaccine refused without physician-documented health risk (§ 5-655). Eligibility requires compliance with annual physical examinations (§ 5-655.01).

condition_specific: Heart disease, hypertension
notes: Covers non-sworn EMS employees of DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Respiratory disease
notes: Covers sworn firefighters with DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Respiratory disease
notes: Covers non-sworn EMS employees of DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, HIV
notes: Requires documented exposure to blood or bodily fluids. Covers sworn firefighters. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence. Coverage denied if eligible vaccine refused without physician-documented health risk (§ 5-655).

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, HIV
notes: Requires documented exposure to blood or bodily fluids. Covers non-sworn EMS employees. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Bladder, blood, brain, breast, cervical, esophageal, intestinal, kidney, lymphatic, lung, prostate, rectal, respiratory tract, skin, testicular, thyroid cancer; leukemia, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
filing_deadline_notes: Claimant must bring claim within 2 years following diagnosis date
notes: Requires 3 years of active service (1,250 hours/year for paid members; active status per department for volunteers). Statute is a benefit fund, not a WC presumption — no rebuttability language. Benefits: early-stage $5,000 lump sum; advanced-stage $20,000 lump sum; combined lifetime max $40,000. Monthly disability: Class 1 (paid active) = lesser of 60% salary or $4,000/month; Class 2 (other eligible) = $1,500/month; up to 36 months duration. Paid and volunteer firefighters both covered; also covers EMTs, paramedics, fire instructors, fire marshals, fire police.

condition_specific: Bladder, blood, brain, breast, cervical, esophageal, intestinal, kidney, lymphatic, lung, prostate, rectal, respiratory tract, skin, testicular, thyroid cancer; leukemia, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
filing_deadline_notes: Claimant must bring claim within 2 years following diagnosis date
notes: Volunteer active status defined per individual department. Requires 3 years active service. Monthly disability benefit for Class 2: $1,500/month up to 36 months. Statute explicitly covers volunteer firefighters.

condition_specific: Bladder, blood, brain, breast, cervical, esophageal, intestinal, kidney, lymphatic, lung, prostate, rectal, respiratory tract, skin, testicular, thyroid cancer; leukemia, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
filing_deadline_notes: Claimant must bring claim within 2 years following diagnosis date
notes: Statute explicitly covers EMTs and paramedics. Requires 3 years active service (1,250 hours/year for paid members). Monthly disability benefit for Class 1 (paid active) = lesser of 60% salary or $4,000/month; Class 2 (other eligible) = $1,500/month.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, breast, cervical, colon, esophageal, invasive skin, kidney, large intestinal, lung, malignant melanoma, mesothelioma, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, oral cavity/pharynx, ovarian, prostate, rectal, stomach, testicular, thyroid cancer
notes: Covers full-time firefighters only (not volunteer). Must not have used tobacco products for at least 5 preceding years. Must not have been employed in a higher-risk position in the preceding 5 years. Benefits include employer-sponsored health plan coverage (with out-of-pocket reimbursement) and $25,000 one-time cash payout on initial diagnosis. Post-termination coverage available for 10 years if eligibility criteria met at termination and not subsequently employed as firefighter. This is an alternative to WC under chapter 440. Effective July 1, 2019.

condition_specific: heart disease, hypertension
notes: Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption that condition was accidental and suffered in the line of duty. Does not apply to life/disability insurance unless negotiated.

condition_specific: heart disease, hypertension
notes: Covers law enforcement officers and correctional officers as defined in s. 943.10(1), (2), or (3). Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal the condition.

condition_specific: tuberculosis
notes: Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption that condition was accidental and suffered in the line of duty.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, non-A, non-B, C, other strains), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Employee must file written affidavit declaring no outside exposure. Pre-employment exam required (testing for hepatitis/TB; meningitis exempt from exam requirement). Employer must maintain exposure records. Immunization refusal disqualifies from presumption. Covers full-time employees only; excludes public hospital employees.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, non-A, non-B, C, other strains), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Covers paramedics and EMTs employed full-time by the state or political subdivision. Written affidavit of no outside exposure required. Immunization refusal disqualifies. Excludes public hospital employees.

condition_specific: tuberculosis
notes: Covers law enforcement officers and correctional officers as defined in s. 943.10(1), (2), or (3). Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal the condition.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, non-A, non-B, C, other strains), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Covers law enforcement and correctional officers employed full-time. Written affidavit of no outside exposure required. Immunization refusal disqualifies. Excludes public hospital employees.

condition_specific: posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be properly noticed within 52 weeks after qualifying event
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist who is an authorized treating physician. Must result from one of enumerated qualifying events (e.g., death of a minor, grievous bodily harm). No physical injury required. Not subject to apportionment for pre-existing PTSD. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer firefighters.

condition_specific: posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be properly noticed within 52 weeks after qualifying event
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease for EMTs and paramedics. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist. Must result from enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer EMTs/paramedics.

condition_specific: posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be properly noticed within 52 weeks after qualifying event
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease for law enforcement officers. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist. Must result from enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer law enforcement.

condition_specific: bladder, blood, brain, breast, cervical, esophageal, intestinal, kidney, lymphatic, lung, prostate, rectum, respiratory tract, skin, testicular, thyroid cancer; leukemia; multiple myeloma; non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Requires 12 consecutive months of service as a firefighter with the same fire department. Provides mandatory insurance coverage (lump sum + monthly disability benefit) rather than a direct WC presumption. Effective January 1, 2018. Volunteers covered under same statute but with lower benefit levels ($1,500/month vs. 60% of salary).

condition_specific: bladder, blood, brain, breast, cervical, esophageal, intestinal, kidney, lymphatic, lung, prostate, rectum, respiratory tract, skin, testicular, thyroid cancer; leukemia; multiple myeloma; non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Volunteers explicitly included in the cancer insurance mandate. Monthly disability benefit is $1,500 (vs. 60% of salary for career firefighters). Effective January 1, 2018. Volunteer fire departments not required to cover volunteers who are simultaneously employed as career firefighters at another department.

condition_specific: heart attack, stroke, vascular rupture
filing_deadline_notes: Must commence within 24 hours after performing work-related activity
notes: Presumption applies if heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture: commenced while performing work-related activity, while on duty after such activity, or not later than 24 hours after such activity; AND directly or proximately resulted in death or disability. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence that it was not related to work activity or was caused solely by cardiovascular disease risk factors.

condition_specific: heart attack, stroke, vascular rupture
filing_deadline_notes: Must commence within 24 hours after performing work-related activity
notes: Statute covers 'public safety officers' defined to include emergency medical technicians. Presumption applies to heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture within 24 hours of work-related activity. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence.

condition_specific: heart attack, stroke, vascular rupture
filing_deadline_notes: Must commence within 24 hours after performing work-related activity
notes: Statute covers 'public safety officers' defined to include law enforcement officers. Presumption applies to heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture within 24 hours of work-related activity. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence.

condition_specific: heart attack, stroke, vascular rupture
filing_deadline_notes: Must commence within 24 hours after performing work-related activity
notes: Statute covers 'public safety officers' defined to include prison guards. Presumption applies to heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture within 24 hours of work-related activity. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence.

condition_specific: leukemia, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin lymphoma, cancer of the lung, brain, stomach, esophagus, intestines, rectum, kidney, bladder, prostate, breast, female reproductive organs, testes
statute_citation: Haw. Rev. Stat. § 386 (medical care for firefighters with cancer)
notes: When a cancer claim is accepted as compensable for a firefighter with 5+ years of service, employer is liable for medical care at 110%-150% of Medicare RBRVS fees applicable to Hawaii. Provision is about enhanced medical benefit level, not a WC presumption per se, but applies once claim is accepted or determined compensable.

condition_specific: disease of the heart
notes: Applies for service-connected disability retirement. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Inhalation of smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes, and other toxic vapors shall be construed as injury received in performance of duty.

condition_specific: disease of the heart
notes: Statute explicitly covers police officers. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition.

condition_specific: disease of the lungs or respiratory system
notes: Applies for service-connected disability retirement. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Inhalation of smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes construed as occupational hazard.

condition_specific: disease of the lungs or respiratory system
notes: Statute explicitly covers police officers. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition.

condition_specific: prostate cancer, primary brain cancer, breast cancer, ovarian cancer, cervical cancer, uterine cancer, malignant melanoma, leukemia, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, bladder cancer, colorectal cancer, multiple myeloma, testicular cancer, kidney cancer
notes: Cancer defined by § 411.1(6). Presumed contracted while on active duty as a result of that duty. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded.

condition_specific: heart disease
notes: Presumed contracted while on active duty as a result of strain. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded. Applies only to Chapter 411 retirement system members (police and firefighters in cities). Covers firefighters who passed regular civil service examination.

condition_specific: any disease of the lungs or respiratory tract
notes: Presumed contracted while on active duty as result of inhalation of noxious fumes, poison, or gases. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded.

condition_specific: HIV/AIDS, all strains of hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, mycobacterium tuberculosis
notes: Infectious disease defined by § 411.1(11). Presumed contracted while on active duty as a result of that duty. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded.

condition_specific: brain cancer (10 yrs), bladder cancer (12 yrs), kidney cancer (15 yrs), colorectal cancer (10 yrs), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (15 yrs), leukemia (5 yrs), mesothelioma (10 yrs), testicular cancer (5 yrs, age <40), breast cancer (5 yrs, age <40), esophageal cancer (10 yrs), multiple myeloma (15 yrs)
filing_deadline_notes: Presumption does not apply to any disease diagnosed more than 10 years following the last date on which the firefighter actually worked as a firefighter
notes: Each cancer type has its own minimum years-of-service threshold (5-15 years). 'Firefighter' = employee whose primary duty is extinguishing or investigating fires. Pre-employment medical screening exam must not have revealed the disease. Presumption is rebuttable by substantial evidence. Post-termination window is 10 years (120 months) from last date worked. Tobacco exclusion: presumption does not apply if firefighter or firefighter's cohabitant has regularly and habitually used tobacco products for 10+ years prior to diagnosis. Testicular cancer: age <40 and no evidence of anabolic steroids or human growth hormone use. Breast cancer: age <40 without breast cancer genetic predisposition.

condition_specific: cardiovascular or pulmonary diseases
notes: Covers paid firemen employed by a municipality, village or fire district as regular members. Caused by overexertion in times of stress or danger OR by proximate exposure OR cumulative exposure over 4+ years to heat, smoke, chemical fumes or toxic gases. Applies to paid firefighters only (not volunteers). Covered diseases are not subject to the limitations of § 72-439.

condition_specific: pulmonary or respiratory diseases
notes: Covers paid firemen employed by municipality, village or fire district as regular members. Caused by overexertion or cumulative exposure over 4+ years to heat, smoke, chemical fumes or toxic gases. Paid firefighters only.

condition_specific: AIDS, AIDS related complexes (ARC), other manifestations of HIV infection, infectious hepatitis viruses, tuberculosis
notes: Applies to occupations involving exposure to human blood or body fluids, which encompasses firefighters. No specific service minimum stated for this provision.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSD or PTSI per DSM-5)
notes: Requires diagnosis by a psychologist, licensed psychiatrist, or counselor trained in PTSI; and clear and convincing evidence the injury was caused by an event or events arising out of employment. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023 (Section 2 of the act), but effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019. Applies to firefighters as defined in §§ 59-1391(f) and 72-1403(A).

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSD or PTSI per DSM-5)
notes: Applies to EMS providers certified under §§ 56-1011 through 56-1018B and ambulance-based clinicians. Requires PTSI diagnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSD or PTSI per DSM-5)
notes: Applies to peace officers (city, county, Idaho state police) as defined in § 19-5101(d). Requires PTSI diagnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019.

condition_specific: disabling cancer that may be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen as defined by IARC
filing_deadline_notes: Cancer must develop or manifest during period while firefighter is in service (pension provisions). 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim (WC/OD Acts).
notes: Cancer type must be one that may be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known IARC carcinogen (4-110.1 and WC/OD Acts). Pre-employment exam: if exam discloses existence of cancer, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no cancer. Article 6 (cities over 500K) requires 7 years.

condition_specific: cancer caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or IARC-defined carcinogen
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

condition_specific: heart disease, stroke
statute_citation: 40 ILCS 5/4-110.1 (municipalities 500,000 and under); 40 ILCS 5/6-151.1 (cities over 500,000); 820 ILCS 305 (Workers' Compensation Act); 820 ILCS 310 (Workers' Occupational Diseases Act)
filing_deadline_notes: Presumption does not apply to employees with less than 5 years at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim with IL WCC (820 ILCS 305/310). Pension provisions require disease to develop/manifest during active service.
notes: Pension provisions (40 ILCS 5/4-110.1 and 6-151.1) require 5 years (Article 4) or 7 years (Article 6) of service, active status, and disease to manifest during service. Article 4 applies to municipalities 500K and under; Article 6 to cities over 500K (7-year minimum). WC/OD Acts (820 ILCS 305/310) require 5 years employment at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim. Pre-employment exam required under pension provisions: if exam shows impairment, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no impairment.

condition_specific: heart or vascular disease or condition, hypertension
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim with IL WCC.
notes: WC/OD Acts cover EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Exclusion: presumption does NOT apply to EMT/A-EMT/paramedic employed by a private employer if the employee spends the preponderance of work time engaged in medical transfers between medical care facilities or non-emergency medical transfers.

condition_specific: tuberculosis, disease of the lungs or respiratory tract
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year employment minimum at time of filing for WC/OD Acts; disease must manifest during active service for pension provisions.
notes: Tuberculosis and lung/respiratory disease covered under all four statutory provisions. Article 4 (5-year minimum), Article 6 (7-year minimum). Pre-employment exam required under pension provisions.

condition_specific: lung or respiratory disease or condition, tuberculosis
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

condition_specific: AIDS, hepatitis C, bloodborne pathogens, MRSA (contagious staph infection)
filing_deadline_notes: Article 6 requires disease during active service. WC/OD Acts require 5 years at time of filing.
notes: AIDS and hepatitis C covered in Article 6 (cities over 500K; 7-year minimum) and WC/OD Acts. MRSA covered under all three WC/OD-related provisions. Pre-employment exam required under Article 6: if exam discloses AIDS, hepatitis C, or MRSA, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no impairment.

condition_specific: bloodborne pathogens, MRSA (contagious staph infection), tuberculosis
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: Section 4-112 provides special protections for firefighters receiving disability pensions for PTSD: mandatory annual medical re-examination may be discontinued if firefighter has reached age 45, has documentation from 2+ physicians approving discontinuance, and board votes affirmatively. Also, no physical or mental disability forming the basis of a pension application may be used as cause for discharge by municipalities or fire protection districts.

condition_specific: hernia, hearing loss
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Hernia and hearing loss explicitly included in the rebuttable presumption under both the Workers' Compensation Act and the Workers' Occupational Diseases Act.

condition_specific: hernia, hearing loss
filing_deadline_notes: 5-year minimum at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim.
notes: Hernia and hearing loss explicitly included for EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

condition_specific: exposure-related cancer — cancer caused by a known carcinogen to which an individual is at risk for occupational exposure (as recognized by IARC or NIOSH)
filing_deadline_notes: Former employee must have terminated employment not more than 60 months (5 years) before diagnosis; individual must be either actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months earlier at time of diagnosis.
notes: Applies to full-time employees of state or political subdivision. Tobacco exclusion: chapter does not apply to any individual who used tobacco products in any form in the 5 years before diagnosis. At time of diagnosis: must be actively employed OR have terminated employment not more than 60 months earlier. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related cancer — cancer caused by a known carcinogen to which an individual is at risk for occupational exposure
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Applies to full-time emergency medical services providers as defined in IC 16-41-10-1. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use in the 5 years before diagnosis. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related cancer — cancer caused by a known carcinogen to which an individual is at risk for occupational exposure
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Applies to full-time members of a police department as defined in IC 36-8-1-9. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use in the 5 years before diagnosis. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related heart or lung disease — disease or impairment of cardiovascular system caused by occupational exposure
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time firefighters only. Tobacco exclusion: chapter does not apply to individuals who used tobacco in the 5 years before diagnosis. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related heart or lung disease — disease or impairment of cardiovascular system
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time EMS providers. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related heart or lung disease — disease or impairment of cardiovascular system
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time law enforcement. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related lung disease — disease or impairment of respiratory system caused by occupational exposure
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time firefighters only. Tobacco exclusion applies (no tobacco use in 5 years before diagnosis). Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related lung disease — disease or impairment of respiratory system
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time EMS providers. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related lung disease — disease or impairment of respiratory system
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time law enforcement. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: mental illness / mental health disability (including PTSD)
notes: Applies to 1977 Fund members (firefighters under IC 36-8-8). Mental health disability treated as Class 1 impairment (same as occupational disease). Mental health disability review panel process: initial 2-year provisional period with required active treatment; panel reevaluates at conclusion; second 2-year provisional period if still impaired; permanent disability after second evaluation if still impaired. Applies to final determinations after December 31, 2012. Employer pays for mental health care during both provisional periods. This is a pension/disability benefit structure, not a WC presumption per se.

condition_specific: mental illness / mental health disability (including PTSD)
notes: Applies to 1977 Fund members (police officers and firefighters under IC 36-8-8). Review panel includes active or retired police officer or firefighter depending on the fund member being evaluated. Same provisional period structure as for firefighters. Employer pays for mental health care during provisional periods.

condition_specific: exposure-related Parkinson's disease — caused by a toxin or head trauma known to increase risk for Parkinson's disease
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Parkinson's disease explicitly added as a presumptive condition (IC 5-10-15-5.5 added by P.L.59-2009). Must be caused by toxin or head trauma known to increase Parkinson's risk AND to which individual is at risk for occupational exposure. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related Parkinson's disease
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time EMS providers. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: exposure-related Parkinson's disease
filing_deadline_notes: Must be actively employed or have terminated not more than 60 months before diagnosis.
notes: Full-time law enforcement. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: cancer which may result from exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen
notes: Cancer presumption limited to types that 'may, in general, result from exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen.' Same re-entry restriction applies. Part of KPERS pension system.

condition_specific: cancer which may result from exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen
notes: Same statute covers 'policeman or fire fighter.' Cancer limited to types resulting from heat, radiation, or known carcinogen exposure. Part of KPERS pension system.

condition_specific: heart disease
notes: Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. If member ceased being a contributing member due to service-connected disability for 6+ months and then rejoined, the presumption for heart/lung/cancer does not apply again until re-contributing for 2 years or clear evidence of duty-connection. Presumption is part of pension/retirement system (KPERS), not standalone WC statute. Burden shifts to member or other party once employer presents contrary evidence.

condition_specific: heart disease
notes: Same statute covers 'policeman or fire fighter.' Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. Re-entry restriction after service-connected disability gap of 6+ months (must re-contribute 2 years before presumption re-activates). Part of KPERS pension system.

condition_specific: disease of the lung or respiratory tract
notes: Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. Same re-entry restriction as cardiovascular row. Part of KPERS pension system.

condition_specific: disease of the lung or respiratory tract
notes: Same statute covers 'policeman or fire fighter.' Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. Part of KPERS pension system.

condition_specific: bladder cancer, brain cancer, colon cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, kidney cancer, liver cancer, lymphatic or haematopoietic cancer, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, skin cancer, cervical cancer, breast cancer
filing_deadline_notes: Cancer must develop and cause death within 10 years of separation from service
notes: CRITICAL: This provision creates a line-of-duty DEATH BENEFIT presumption only — the statute explicitly states it does NOT create a WC presumption under KRS Chapter 342 and shall not be construed to do so. Conditions: firefighter for at least 5 consecutive years; cancer-related death within 10 years of separation; no tobacco use for 10 years prior to diagnosis; under age 65 at time of death; no pre-employment cancer diagnosis; exposed to IARC or NTP recognized carcinogen reasonably associated with the cancer. Rebuttability not explicitly stated for this benefit.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers full-time, career, and volunteer firefighters. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist, psychologist, or licensed professional counselor (KRS 335.500–335.599). PTSD must be caused by an event or accumulation of events in course and scope of employment. NOTE: This provision is a mental health treatment reimbursement program (out-of-pocket costs reimbursed by commission), not a traditional WC compensability presumption — maximum 12 months of reimbursement. Personnel action exclusion applies (transfer, promotion, termination not compensable).

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Explicitly covers volunteer firefighters. Same diagnosis and reimbursement structure as career firefighters. Mental health treatment reimbursement program, not WC compensability presumption. Maximum 12 months reimbursement.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, colon, liver, pancreas, skin, kidney, gastrointestinal or reproductive tract cancers, leukemia, lymphoma, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, and any other cancer with statistically significant increased occupational risk
post_termination_formula: 3 months per full year of service, max 60 months (pre-2023 termination); 120 months for qualifying long-service members (post-2023)
filing_deadline_notes: For firefighters terminating on or after Jan 1, 2023: 120-month post-termination window applies only to: (a) 10+ years service attaining age 55; (b) 20+ years service attaining age 50; (c) 25+ years service any age; (d) 10+ years service approved for disability retirement. Diagnosis must be prior to age 65 for post-2023 terminations.
notes: Covers firefighters in classified service with 10+ years. Cancer limited to types caused by heat, smoke, radiation, or IARC-recognized known/suspected carcinogens. Two-tier post-termination window: pre-2023 terminations get 3 months/year up to 60 months; post-2023 terminations get 120 months for qualifying members (long service + age criteria). Age 65 cap on diagnosis for post-2023 terminations.

condition_specific: disease or infirmity of the heart
notes: Covers classified fire service. Presumption is prima facie for disease/infirmity manifested any time after the first 5 years of employment. No explicit post-termination window stated. Member need not be on duty when stricken. Statute is separate from and does not affect R.S. 33:2011 (cancer) provisions.

condition_specific: disease or infirmity of the lungs
notes: Same statute as cardiovascular row (R.S. 33:2581). Covers lung disease alongside heart disease in classified fire service. Presumption is prima facie after 5 years of employment. No explicit post-termination window stated.

condition_specific: Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C
post_termination_formula: 3 months per full year of service, max 60 months
filing_deadline_notes: Post-termination window commences with last actual date of service
notes: Covers firefighters in classified service with 2+ years of service. Rebuttable by: (1) evidence meeting judicial standards, or (2) evidence that within 1 year of diagnosis member unlawfully used IV controlled substances, or lived in intimate relationship with a person diagnosed with Hep B or C.

condition_specific: Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C
post_termination_formula: 3 months per full year of service, max 60 months
filing_deadline_notes: Post-termination window commences with last actual date of service
notes: Explicitly covers 'policeman in the classified service' with 2+ years of service alongside firefighters. Same rebuttability conditions as firefighter row.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers 'fire employee' (full-time regularly paid fire department employment) and volunteer firemen. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after employment. Prima facie presumption of service-connection. Personnel action exclusion applies. Does not modify pension/retirement eligibility calculations.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers volunteer members of fire companies under WC coverage per R.S. 23:1036. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after volunteer service. Prima facie presumption of service-connection. Personnel action exclusion (transfer, promotion, demotion, termination not compensable).

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Explicitly covers 'emergency medical services personnel' as defined in R.S. 40:1075.3 employed pursuant to this Chapter. Same prima facie presumption structure as firefighters. Diagnosis during or after employment. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Explicitly covers 'employee of a police department' as defined in R.S. 33:2211. Same prima facie presumption structure. Diagnosis during or after employment. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers employees of the Louisiana Division of State Police (separate statute from R.S. 33:2581.2 which covers municipal police). Diagnosis by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after employment. Prima facie presumption for WC purposes. Personnel action exclusion applies. Does not modify pension/retirement eligibility.

condition_specific: hearing loss (10% greater than comparable age group in general population)
filing_deadline_notes: Post-termination coverage extends 24 months from termination
notes: Covers classified fire service. Hearing loss must be 10% greater than the affected employee's comparable age group in the general population. Entitlement is to medical benefits (including hearing prosthesis) only — not full disability benefits. Baseline audiology exam required for new hires on or after July 1, 2006 (within 1 year of appointment). Presumption applies after 5 years of service. 24-month post-termination window.

condition_specific: cancer affecting skin or central nervous, lymphatic, digestive, hematological, urinary, skeletal, oral, or prostate systems, lung or respiratory tract; any cancer linked to heat, radiation, or IARC-listed carcinogen with statistically significant correlation to fire service
filing_deadline_notes: Any person first discovering such condition within 5 years of the last date on which such person actively served is eligible to apply.
notes: Covers uniformed members of paid fire departments, members of state police assigned to fire investigation unit of Department of Fire Services, state police K-9 unit members, and Logan Airport crash crewmen/boatmen/fire controlmen. Must result in total disability or death. Requires: (1) 5 years minimum service; (2) cancer type must be one that may result from heat, radiation, or IARC-listed carcinogen; (3) member must have regularly responded to fire calls or investigation at scene. Pre-employment or subsequent exam must not have revealed the condition. Post-termination window: discovery within 5 years of last active service date. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence of non-service-connected risk factors or hazards.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
notes: Covers uniformed members of paid fire departments, including permanent crash crewmen, crash boatmen, fire controlmen, and assistant fire controlmen at Logan International Airport. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
notes: Covers permanent members of police departments (including park police), MBTA police force, state police, and public works building police. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
notes: Covers employees in the Department of Correction or county correctional facility whose regular or incidental duties require care, supervision, or custody of prisoners, criminally insane persons, or defective delinquents. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: disease of the lungs or respiratory tract
notes: Covers uniformed members of paid fire departments, including crash crewmen, crash boatmen, fire controlmen, and assistant fire controlmen at Logan International Airport. Must result in total disability or death. Presumed to result from inhalation of noxious fumes or poisonous gases. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of such condition. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: leukemia, prostate, rectal, throat, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, brain, testicular, bladder, kidney/renal cell, thyroid, colon, ovarian, breast cancer
notes: Requires: (1) cancer caused by contact with toxic substance encountered in the line of duty; (2) at least 10 years of service as firefighter, fire fighting instructor, rescue squad member, or advanced life support unit member; (3) unable to perform normal duties due to cancer. Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, and sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal.

condition_specific: leukemia, prostate, rectal, throat, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, brain, testicular, bladder, kidney/renal cell, thyroid, colon, ovarian, breast cancer
notes: Covers volunteer firefighters, volunteer fire fighting instructors, volunteer rescue squad members, and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required. Requires 10 years of service; cancer must be caused by toxic substance in line of duty; must be unable to perform normal duties.

condition_specific: heart disease, hypertension
notes: Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal employed by airport authority, county, fire control district, municipality, or State. Hypertension sub-provision (§9-503(a)(2)) requires: hypertension diagnosis by authorized provider, medication prescribed for at least 90 consecutive days, at least 2 years cumulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: heart disease, hypertension
notes: Covers volunteer firefighters, volunteer fire fighting instructors, volunteer rescue squad members, and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required. Hypertension expanded provision (§9-503(a)(2)) applies only to paid members.

condition_specific: heart disease, hypertension
notes: Covers paid rescue squad members and paid advanced life support unit members. Hypertension sub-provision (§9-503(a)(2)) requires: diagnosis by authorized provider, medication for at least 90 consecutive days, at least 2 years cumulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.

condition_specific: lung disease
notes: Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal employed by airport authority, county, fire control district, municipality, or State.

condition_specific: lung disease
notes: Covers volunteer rescue squad members and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required.

condition_specific: lung disease
notes: Covers paid rescue squad members and paid advanced life support unit members.

condition_specific: kidney cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, colon cancer, leukemia, brain cancer, bladder cancer, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, breast cancer, gynecologic cancer
filing_deadline_notes: Diagnosis must be within 10 years of last active employment as firefighter OR prior to attaining age 70, whichever occurs first
notes: Covers municipal fire department members and volunteer fire association members (via 'employed' definition that includes volunteer members with no compensation other than injury/death benefits). Minimum 5 years employment; must have regularly responded to firefighting or emergency calls (exception for State Fire Marshal investigators/sergeants and forest protection employees). Pre-employment test required (must test negative for cancer). Firefighter must sign affidavit that cancer is not prevalent in blood-related family and no substantial lifetime carcinogen exposure other than through firefighting. State Fire Marshal investigators/sergeants must certify use of protective equipment per departmental policies.

condition_specific: kidney cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, colon cancer, leukemia, brain cancer, bladder cancer, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, breast cancer, gynecologic cancer
filing_deadline_notes: Diagnosis must be within 10 years of last active employment as firefighter OR prior to attaining age 70, whichever occurs first
notes: 'Employed' definition in Sec. 328-B explicitly includes volunteer fire association members with no compensation other than injury/death benefits. Same 5-year service, pre-employment test, affidavit, and post-termination window requirements.

condition_specific: cardiovascular injury or disease
filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Covers active members of municipal fire departments AND volunteer firefighters associations. Firefighter defined broadly to include administrative/other duty members who aid in fire extinguishment. 2-year membership minimum required. Temporal trigger: disease/injury within 6 months of actual firefighting or firefighting training/drill.

condition_specific: cardiovascular injury or disease
filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Volunteer firefighters association members explicitly included in 'firefighter' definition under Sec. 328. Same 2-year membership and 6-month temporal trigger requirements.

condition_specific: pulmonary disease
filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Same statute as cardiovascular row (Sec. 328). Pulmonary disease covered alongside cardiovascular. Same 2-year minimum and 6-month firefighting-activity trigger.

condition_specific: pulmonary disease
filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or injury must occur within 6 months of participating in fire fighting, or training/drill that actually involves fire fighting
notes: Volunteer firefighters association members explicitly included. Same conditions as career firefighter row.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Covers 'emergency rescue or public safety workers' with high risk of occupational exposure. Firefighter defined as active member of municipal fire department or volunteer fire association. Pre-employment physical required (if employer requires it) — must test negative for hepatitis/TB. Worker must sign affidavit declaring no outside exposure sources, no transfusions, no high-risk sexual behavior, no IV drug use. Preemployment test exemption for workers employed on the effective date of the section. Multi-employer rule: last employer of injurious exposure is liable. Presumption explicitly applies for disability retirement purposes.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Volunteer fire association members explicitly included in firefighter definition under Sec. 328-A. Employer includes entities for which person provides volunteer services. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization requirements.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: EMS person defined as licensed under Title 32, ch. 2-B, employed by or providing voluntary service to a licensed ambulance service or nontransporting EMS. Must run high risk of occupational exposure. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Law enforcement officer as defined in Title 25, sec. 2801-A, sub. 5. Must run high risk of occupational exposure. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, C, or any generally recognized strain), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Corrections officer as defined in Title 25, sec. 2801-A, sub. 2. Must run high risk of occupational exposure through custody/restraint duties. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence. Diagnosis must be by allopathic or osteopathic physician (Title 32, ch. 48 or 36) with psychiatry specialization, or licensed psychologist (Title 32, ch. 56). Personnel action exclusion: PTSD not compensable if resulting from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination, or similar good-faith employer action. Firefighter defined as in Sec. 328-A.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Emergency medical services worker as defined in Sec. 328-A. Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Law enforcement officer as defined in Sec. 328-A (Title 25, sec. 2801-A). Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Corrections officer as defined in Sec. 328-A (Title 25, sec. 2801-A). Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: respiratory tract, bladder, skin, brain, kidney, blood, thyroid, testicular, prostate, lymphatic, ovarian, breast, non-HPV cervical cancer
statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(2)
notes: Covers full-time fire department members (all service lengths) and, beginning January 1, 2022 for cancers diagnosed on or after January 1, 2022, part-time, paid on-call, and volunteer members. Also covers former members from any of those categories. Requires 60 months (5 years) active service at time of cancer manifestation. Member must have been exposed to hazards incidental to fire suppression, rescue, or EMS duties. Claim is made against the Christopher R. Slezak First Responder Presumed Coverage Fund (not the employer) — employer claim is suspended during fund benefit receipt. Presumption applies only to claims against the Fund. Tobacco rebuttal: rebuttable by scientific evidence that claimant was a substantial and consistent user of tobacco products within 10 years preceding injury and that use was a significant factor in cancer cause/aggravation/progression. Pension application required as condition precedent.

condition_specific: respiratory tract, bladder, skin, brain, kidney, blood, thyroid, testicular, prostate, lymphatic, ovarian, breast, non-HPV cervical cancer
statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(2)
notes: Volunteer members (and former volunteer members) eligible for cancer presumption beginning January 1, 2022 for cancers diagnosed on or after January 1, 2022. Requires 60 months (5 years) active service at time of cancer manifestation. Exposure to fire suppression/rescue/EMS hazards required. Claims go against the Christopher R.
