Slezak First Responder Presumed Coverage Fund; fund is considered employer for volunteer members. Forest fire officers and fire/crash rescue officers also covered for same cancer list. Tobacco rebuttal provision applies. Pension application required as condition precedent.

condition_specific: heart disease, illnesses resulting from heart disease
statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid fire departments (city, township, village, airport county/public authority/state university, or public fire authority). Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary (§418.405(3)). As condition precedent, claimant must first apply for pension benefits or demonstrate ineligibility.

condition_specific: heart disease, illnesses resulting from heart disease
statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid police departments (city, township, village), county sheriffs and deputies, members of state police, conservation officers, and motor carrier enforcement division officers. Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary. Pension application required as condition precedent.

condition_specific: respiratory diseases, illnesses resulting from respiratory diseases
statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid fire departments (city, township, village, airport county/public authority/state university, or public fire authority). Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary (§418.405(3)). As condition precedent, claimant must first apply for pension benefits or demonstrate ineligibility.

condition_specific: respiratory diseases, illnesses resulting from respiratory diseases
statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid police departments, county sheriffs and deputies, state police members, conservation officers, and motor carrier enforcement division officers. Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary. Pension application required as condition precedent.

condition_specific: disabling cancer caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or IARC-listed known or suspected carcinogen reasonably linked to the disabling cancer
notes: Covers firefighters on active duty with an organized fire department who are unable to perform duties due to disabling cancer. Cancer type must be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or IARC-listed carcinogen, and the carcinogen must be reasonably linked to the disabling cancer. Exception: if firefighter entered service after August 1, 1988, and pre-hire exam disclosed existing cancer, presumption does not apply unless subsequent medical determination finds cancer no longer present.

condition_specific: myocarditis, coronary sclerosis
notes: Covers active duty members of organized fire departments of any municipality. Pre-employment thorough physical examination by licensed physician required; written report must be filed with department; exam must have been negative for the disease at time of employment. Disease must manifest at date of disablement or death while employed on active duty. Statute uses 'presumptively an occupational disease' language with no express rebuttal provision for this subsection — treated as irrebuttable here.

condition_specific: myocarditis, coronary sclerosis
notes: Covers active duty members of organized police departments of any municipality, Minnesota State Patrol members, conservation officers, state crime bureau members, forest officers (DNR), state correctional officers, and sheriffs/full-time deputy sheriffs. Pre-employment physical exam required; written report filed; exam must have been negative. No express rebuttal provision for this subsection.

condition_specific: pneumonia or its sequel
notes: Covers active duty members of organized fire departments of any municipality. Pre-employment thorough physical examination by licensed physician required; written report must be filed with department; exam must have been negative for the disease. Disease must manifest at date of disablement or death while employed on active duty. No express rebuttal provision for this subsection.

condition_specific: pneumonia or its sequel
notes: Same coverage and conditions as firefighter respiratory row. Covers state patrol, police, conservation officers, crime bureau, DNR forest officers, correctional officers, sheriffs/deputies. Pre-employment physical exam required; written report filed; exam must have been negative. No express rebuttal provision.

condition_specific: infectious or communicable disease contracted through occupational exposure
notes: Covers licensed firefighters. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors brought by employer or insurer; any substantial factors known at time of denial must be communicated to employee on denial of liability.

condition_specific: infectious or communicable disease contracted through occupational exposure
notes: Covers paramedics and emergency medical technicians. Covers individuals who by nature of their position provide emergency medical care, including licensed nurses providing emergency care. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

condition_specific: infectious or communicable disease contracted through occupational exposure
notes: Covers licensed police officers under § 626.84, subd. 1. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

condition_specific: infectious or communicable disease contracted through occupational exposure
notes: Covers state correctional officers. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) as diagnosed by licensed psychiatrist or psychologist per current DSM
notes: Mental impairment defined as PTSD diagnosis by licensed psychiatrist or psychologist per current DSM. Employee must not have been previously diagnosed with the condition. PTSD is NOT an occupational disease if resulting from: disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, promotion, termination, retirement, or similar good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) as diagnosed by licensed psychiatrist or psychologist per current DSM
notes: Covers paramedics, emergency medical technicians, and licensed nurses employed to provide emergency medical services outside of a medical facility. Also covers public safety dispatchers. Must not have been previously diagnosed with the condition. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary or good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) as diagnosed by licensed psychiatrist or psychologist per current DSM
notes: Covers licensed police officers and members of the Minnesota State Patrol and full-time deputy sheriffs/sheriffs. Must not have been previously diagnosed. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary or good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) as diagnosed by licensed psychiatrist or psychologist per current DSM
notes: Covers officers employed by the state or a political subdivision at a corrections, detention, or secure treatment facility. Must not have been previously diagnosed. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary or good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors.

condition_specific: Cancer affecting skin, central nervous, lymphatic, digestive, hematological, urinary, skeletal, oral, breast, testicular, genitourinary, liver, prostate systems; cancer from heat, radiation, or IARC-identified carcinogen exposure
filing_deadline_notes: Physical examination must have been within 5 years prior to the claim and must have failed to reveal evidence of cancer
notes: Tobacco exclusion: presumption defeated if proven to reasonable medical certainty that condition was not contributed to by voluntary tobacco use. Applies to uniformed members of paid fire departments of all counties, cities, towns, fire districts, and other governmental units. § 320.400 establishes a separate voluntary firefighter cancer benefits pool (not a WC presumption; benefits reduced 25% for tobacco use within 5 years of diagnosis).

condition_specific: Hypertension, disease of the heart, hypotension (§ 87.006 only)
filing_deadline_notes: Physical examination must have been within 5 years prior to the claim and must have failed to reveal evidence of the condition
notes: § 87.005 covers hypertension and heart disease; § 87.006 additionally covers hypotension. § 87.005 applies to chapter 87 retirement provisions; § 87.006 applies to all paid fire departments statewide.

condition_specific: Disease of the lungs or respiratory tract
filing_deadline_notes: Physical examination must have been within 5 years prior to the claim and must have failed to reveal evidence of the condition
notes: § 87.005 applies to chapter 87 retirement provisions only; § 87.006 applies to all paid fire departments statewide. Both require pre-employment exam within 5 years.

condition_specific: HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, hepatitis C, hepatitis D, diphtheria, meningococcal meningitis, MRSA, hemorrhagic fever, plague, rabies, SARS
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be made within 5 years of the physical examination that failed to reveal the condition; annual physical with blood test required for infectious disease presumption
notes: § 87.005 applies only to chapter 87 RSMo 1959 retirement/relief provisions; § 87.006 applies to all county/city/town/fire district paid fire department members for retirement benefit computation. Both require pre-employment exam within 5 years prior to claim. Annual blood test required for infectious disease presumption.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, colon, liver, pancreas, skin, kidney, gastrointestinal tract, reproductive tract, leukemia, lymphoma, multiple myeloma, prostate, testicles, breast cancer
statute_citation: Mississippi First Responders Health and Safety Act ("Arson Investigator Danny Benton and Police Chief Henry Manuel, Sr., Act"), Section 2-7; Mississippi Code § 71-3-9
notes: Effective July 1, 2021. This is a separate cancer benefits program, structured as an alternative to workers' compensation (§71-3-9 amended so employer not liable under WC if first responder elects this Act). Benefits include lump sums ($35,000 for malignant tumors meeting specific criteria; $6,250 for carcinoma in situ or endoscopic-treated malignancies; maximum $50,000 lifetime), plus disability benefit of 60% of monthly salary or $5,000/month max for nonvolunteers, beginning 6 months after disability, for 36 monthly payments. Requires a board-certified physician in the appropriate specialty to certify cancer was caused by an occupational hazard. Employer bears cost; claimant choosing this program forfeits WC claim for same diagnosis. Covers full-time firefighters with 10+ years of service employed by State of Mississippi or any political subdivision.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, colon, liver, pancreas, skin, kidney, gastrointestinal tract, reproductive tract, leukemia, lymphoma, multiple myeloma, prostate, testicles, breast cancer
statute_citation: Mississippi First Responders Health and Safety Act ("Arson Investigator Danny Benton and Police Chief Henry Manuel, Sr., Act"), Section 2-7; Mississippi Code § 71-3-9
notes: Effective July 1, 2021. Covers volunteer firefighters registered with the State of Mississippi or a political subdivision with 10+ years of service. Disability benefit for volunteers is $1,500/month (not 60% of salary), beginning 6 months after disability, for 36 monthly payments. Same lump-sum benefit structure applies ($35,000/$6,250, max $50,000 lifetime). Board-certified physician must certify occupational causation. Electing this program forfeits WC claim for same diagnosis.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, colon, liver, pancreas, skin, kidney, gastrointestinal tract, reproductive tract, leukemia, lymphoma, multiple myeloma, prostate, testicles, breast cancer
statute_citation: Mississippi First Responders Health and Safety Act ("Arson Investigator Danny Benton and Police Chief Henry Manuel, Sr., Act"), Section 2-7; Mississippi Code § 71-3-9
notes: Effective July 1, 2021. Covers law enforcement officers certified by the Mississippi Board on Law Enforcement Officer Standards and Training (BLEOST) with 10+ years of service. Same benefit structure as firefighters: lump-sum up to $50,000 lifetime, disability benefit of 60% of salary or $5,000/month max for 36 months beginning 6 months after disability. Board-certified physician must certify occupational causation. Electing this program forfeits WC claim for same diagnosis.

condition_specific: Bladder cancer (12 yrs), brain cancer (10 yrs), breast cancer (5 yrs, age <40, no genetic predisposition), cervical cancer (15 yrs), colorectal cancer (10 yrs), esophageal cancer (10 yrs), kidney cancer (15 yrs), leukemia (5 yrs), lung cancer (4 yrs), mesothelioma/asbestosis (10 yrs), multiple myeloma (15 yrs), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (15 yrs), testicular cancer (10 yrs)
post_termination_formula: Disease must manifest within 10 years of last date engaged in firefighting activities; individual disease minimums range from 4–15 years of employment
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed as soon as firefighter knows or should have known condition resulted from presumptive occupational disease
notes: Tobacco exclusion: firefighter may not be a regular user of tobacco products, must not have a history of regular tobacco use in 10 years preceding claim, and must not have been exposed by a cohabitant who regularly used tobacco in the home for 10+ years before diagnosis. Employment history calculated from July 1, 2014 only (§ 39-71(3)). Volunteer firefighters covered only if employer opts in AND insurer agrees to provide presumptive coverage (§ 39-71(1)(b)). Total claim payment capped at $5 million per claim. Pre-employment exam required within 90 days of hiring; subsequent exams at least every 2 years. Lung cancer and myocardial infarction must be the type that can be caused by firefighting activities. Effective July 1, 2019; applies to diseases diagnosed on or after that date.

condition_specific: Bladder cancer (12 yrs), brain cancer (10 yrs), breast cancer (5 yrs, age <40, no genetic predisposition), cervical cancer (15 yrs), colorectal cancer (10 yrs), esophageal cancer (10 yrs), kidney cancer (15 yrs), leukemia (5 yrs), lung cancer (4 yrs), mesothelioma/asbestosis (10 yrs), multiple myeloma (15 yrs), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (15 yrs), testicular cancer (10 yrs)
post_termination_formula: Disease must manifest within 10 years of last date engaged in firefighting activities; individual disease minimums range from 4–15 years of employment
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed as soon as firefighter knows or should have known condition resulted from presumptive occupational disease
notes: Coverage for volunteer firefighters is OPTIONAL — applies only if employer has elected WC coverage under Title 39, chapter 71, AND the employer has additionally opted to include presumptive occupational disease coverage, AND the insurer agrees to provide it. At least 1 year of firefighting operations in Montana beginning on or after July 1, 2019 required. Same conditions, service minimums, and tobacco exclusion as paid firefighter rows.

condition_specific: Myocardial infarction
post_termination_formula: Disease must manifest within 10 years of last date engaged in firefighting activities
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed as soon as firefighter knows or should have known condition resulted from presumptive occupational disease
notes: Myocardial infarction listed in the statute alongside cancers as a presumptive occupational disease. Must be the type that can be caused by firefighting activities. Same tobacco exclusion and exam requirements as cancer rows. Volunteer firefighters covered only if employer opts in. Employment history calculated from July 1, 2014 only.

condition_specific: Myocardial infarction
post_termination_formula: Disease must manifest within 10 years of last date engaged in firefighting activities
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed as soon as firefighter knows or should have known condition resulted from presumptive occupational disease
notes: Volunteer firefighter coverage is optional (employer and insurer opt-in required). Same conditions and exclusions as paid firefighter cardiovascular row.

condition_specific: mesothelioma, testicular cancer, intestinal cancer, esophageal cancer
notes: This is a line-of-duty DEATH BENEFIT presumption only (Public Safety Employees' Death Benefits Act), not a workers' compensation presumption. Death from one of the four listed cancers is presumed to be occupationally related to firefighting and therefore a line-of-duty death, triggering a $100,000 death benefit to dependents. Cancers must be 'occupationally related to firefighting.' No rebuttability provision stated.

condition_specific: malignant neoplasms caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen as defined by WHO/IARC
statute_citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. §§ 58-86A-1 through 58-86A-6 (Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program)
filing_deadline_notes: Must have a new diagnosis of cancer on or after January 1, 2022; prior diagnosed cancer type ineligible, but new cancer types diagnosed on or after Jan 1, 2022 remain eligible even if metastasized from earlier cancer
notes: This is a supplemental state insurance benefits program, NOT a workers' compensation presumption. Benefits are supplemental to other health benefits. Firefighter is ineligible if simultaneously receiving WC benefits under Chapter 97 for the same cancer. Eligibility: 5 continuous years of service in an NC fire department (lapse of up to 6 months does not break continuity), OR on NC State Firefighters' Association certified roster as 'retired/nonactive' for no more than 10 years post-service. Benefits: lump sum up to $74,000 total ($37,000 per diagnosis); disability benefit of 75% of monthly salary or $5,000/month max (whichever less) for non-volunteers, or $1,500/month for volunteers, beginning 6 months after total disability, for up to 36 consecutive months. Disability benefit subordinate to other disability benefits from other sources (except private insurance purchased solely by the firefighter). Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered. Administered by the Office of the State Fire Marshal via a contracted third-party administrator.

condition_specific: malignant neoplasms caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen as defined by WHO/IARC
statute_citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. §§ 58-86A-1 through 58-86A-6 (Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program)
filing_deadline_notes: Must have a new diagnosis of cancer on or after January 1, 2022
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly included in the Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program (§ 58-86A-4(2)(b)). Disability benefit for volunteers is $1,500/month (not salary-based) for up to 36 consecutive months beginning 6 months after total disability. Lump-sum benefit structure same as non-volunteers ($37,000 per diagnosis, max $74,000 total). Same eligibility requirements apply (5 continuous years of service). Ineligible if simultaneously receiving WC benefits for the same cancer.

condition_specific: occupational cancer arising from exposure to smoke, fumes, carcinogenic/poisonous/toxic/chemical substances
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Cancer presumption applies to firefighters only (not law enforcement). Cancer must arise out of employment due to exposure to smoke, fumes, or carcinogenic/poisonous/toxic/chemical substances in active duty.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Tobacco users must provide yearly physician documentation of tobacco-free status for preceding 2 years to remain eligible. Separate cardiac event presumption in § 65-01-15.2 applies when event occurs within 48 hours of strenuous activity or emergency response.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Separate cardiac event presumption in § 65-01-15.2 applies when event occurs within 48 hours of strenuous law enforcement activity or emergency response; rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence.

condition_specific: lung or respiratory disease
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Tobacco users must provide yearly physician documentation of tobacco-free status for preceding 2 years to remain eligible. Periodic medical exams required by employer: every 5 years (1–10 yrs service), every 3 years (11–20 yrs), annually (21+ yrs). Medical exam results used in rebuttal.

condition_specific: lung or respiratory disease
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Law enforcement officer defined as licensed peace officer employed full-time by specified agencies (BCI, game and fish, state highway patrol, parole/probation, university police, county sheriff, city PD). Cancer presumption does NOT apply to law enforcement.

condition_specific: bloodborne pathogens (hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV)
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Bloodborne pathogen exposure must occur in the course of employment. Tobacco exclusion provision explicitly does not apply to bloodborne pathogen exposure claims.

condition_specific: bloodborne pathogens (hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV)
post_termination_formula: 2 years post-employment for those employed ≤10 years; 5 years post-employment for those employed >10 years
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must be diagnosed within 2 years after employment ends (if ≤10 years service) or within 5 years after employment ends (if >10 years service)
notes: Bloodborne pathogen exposure must occur in the course of employment. Tobacco exclusion provision explicitly does not apply to bloodborne pathogen exposure claims.

condition_specific: Cancer affecting skin, central nervous, lymphatic, digestive, hematological, urinary, skeletal, oral, prostate systems; including melanoma (Firefighter Cancer Benefits Act); breast, lung, neurological, reproductive system (FCBA)
statute_citation: Neb. Rev. Stat. § 35-1001(1); Firefighter Cancer Benefits Act (LB 44)
notes: § 35-1001(1) provides prima facie evidence (rebuttable) for paid fire department members; requires exposure to IARC-identified known carcinogen while in service and carcinogen is suspected/known cause of the type of cancer. Pre-employment exam required that failed to reveal cancer. Post-separation window is 3 months (§ 35-1001(3)). Firefighter Cancer Benefits Act (FCBA) provides enhanced cash benefits for paid firefighters and volunteers deemed employees; requires 24 consecutive months service, participation in fire suppression, and use of all available PPE. FCBA post-separation eligibility: 36 months. Firefighters already provided cancer benefits under § 35-1001 are ineligible for FCBA (§ Sec. 4(3)). FCBA benefit levels: $25,000 lump sum for advanced/terminal cancer, $6,250 for carcinoma in situ/melanoma, $1,500/month disability benefit up to 36 months, $50,000 death benefit.

condition_specific: Cancer affecting skin, central nervous, lymphatic, digestive, hematological, urinary, skeletal, oral, prostate systems
notes: § 35-1001 explicitly covers 'firefighter-paramedic' as a separate category alongside 'firefighter' — both must be members of a paid fire department. Same requirements as firefighter row: pre-employment exam, IARC carcinogen exposure, 3-month post-separation window.

condition_specific: Hypertension, heart disease
notes: Requires 5 years total service as member of a paid fire department in any city. Rebuttable presumption. Also covers police officers (separate row). 3-month post-separation window. Applies to chapter 15, article 10, §§ 16-1001 to 16-1042, and any firefighter/police pension plans under home rule charters.

condition_specific: Hypertension, heart disease
notes: § 18-1723 covers police officers of any city or village (including home rule charter cities) — no minimum years of service stated for police officers (5-year minimum stated only for firefighters). 3-month post-separation window.

condition_specific: Respiratory defect or disease
notes: § 18-1723 covers heart, hypertension, AND respiratory defect/disease together. 5 years paid service required. 3-month post-separation window.

condition_specific: Respiratory defect or disease
notes: § 18-1723 covers police officers for heart, hypertension, AND respiratory defect/disease. No service minimum stated for police. 3-month post-separation window.

condition_specific: Blood-borne infectious disease (HIV/AIDS, all strains of hepatitis), tuberculosis, meningococcal meningitis, MRSA
filing_deadline_notes: Firefighter must have been engaged in service within 10 years before onset of disease
notes: Requires: (a) pre-employment/subsequent exam that failed to reveal evidence of the disease, AND (b) firefighter engaged in service within 10 years before disease onset. Post-separation window: 3 months (§ 35-1001(3)). Applies to paid fire departments only.

condition_specific: Blood-borne infectious disease (HIV/AIDS, all strains of hepatitis), tuberculosis, meningococcal meningitis, MRSA
filing_deadline_notes: Firefighter-paramedic must have been engaged in service within 10 years before onset of disease
notes: § 35-1001 explicitly covers 'firefighter-paramedic' alongside 'firefighter.' Same requirements as firefighter infectious row.

condition_specific: Mental injuries and mental illness (unaccompanied by physical injury) caused by extraordinary and unusual employment conditions
notes: § 48-101.01 is NOT a presumption — it makes mental injuries compensable for first responders but requires the employee to establish by preponderance of evidence that (a) conditions were extraordinary and unusual compared to normal employment and (b) medical causation. Covers paid and volunteer firefighters. Excluded if resulting from normal employer-employee relations (disciplinary actions, evaluations, transfers, etc.).

condition_specific: Mental injuries and mental illness (unaccompanied by physical injury) caused by extraordinary and unusual employment conditions
notes: § 48-101.01 covers licensed EMS personnel (licensed under § 38-1217) providing immediate medical care. Not a presumption — requires employee proof by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Mental injuries and mental illness (unaccompanied by physical injury) caused by extraordinary and unusual employment conditions
notes: § 48-101.01 covers sheriff, deputy sheriff, police officer, Nebraska State Patrol officer, volunteer and paid firefighter, and licensed EMS. Not a presumption — requires employee proof by preponderance of evidence. Excludes mental injuries from normal employer-employee relations.

condition_specific: cancer disease (occupationally caused)
post_termination_formula: Retired firefighters 6–20 years post-retirement eligible for medical payments only; must be receiving pension under RSA 100-A and guarantee tobacco-free lifestyle
filing_deadline_notes: Retired firefighters between 6 and 20 years post-retirement eligible for medical expenses only under this section
notes: Prima facie presumption requires 10 years of service. Tobacco-free lifestyle required (no tobacco product 4+ times/week in past 6 months, except Alaska Native/Native American religious/ceremonial use). Two tracks: (1) If dept follows NFPA 1582 exam standard: firefighter provides exam report plus tobacco-free guarantee — gets presumption. (2) If dept does NOT follow NFPA 1582: firefighter must be 10-year tobacco-free veteran AND present after-action reports documenting known carcinogen exposure — does NOT receive prima facie presumption in this case. Employer must have policy following fire standards/training commission curriculum for best practices in PPE use and cleaning. Active regular firefighters employed before Jan 1, 1997 may have NFPA 1582 exam reimbursed by Dept of Safety.

condition_specific: heart disease
post_termination_formula: Retired firefighters: 5 years from retirement date; must agree to submit to requested physicals
filing_deadline_notes: Benefits do not continue beyond one month after firefighter reaches age 65
notes: Prima facie presumption. Applies to regular, call, volunteer, and retired members of a fire department. Call/volunteer firefighters must have reasonable medical evidence on record showing freedom from disease at employment start; employer has duty to provide it, and if employer fails, firefighter still gets presumption. Retired firefighters get presumption for 5 years from retirement date if they agree to submit to requested physicals. Age 65 cutoff: benefits end one month after firefighter's 65th birthday.

condition_specific: lung disease
post_termination_formula: Retired firefighters: 5 years from retirement date; must agree to submit to requested physicals
filing_deadline_notes: Benefits do not continue beyond one month after firefighter reaches age 65
notes: Prima facie presumption for lung disease — same statute and conditions as heart disease presumption (§ 281-A:17(I)). Covers regular, call, volunteer, and retired firefighters. Same medical evidence and age 65 conditions apply.

condition_specific: acute stress disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' under § 281-A:2, V-c includes call, volunteer, and regular firefighters. Injury definition under § 281-A:2, XI was amended to include ASD and PTSD for these workers. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination, or similar good-faith employer action.

condition_specific: acute stress disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' definition under § 281-A:2, V-c includes rescue or ambulance workers: ambulance service, emergency medical personnel, first responder service, and volunteer personnel.

condition_specific: acute stress disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' definition under § 281-A:2, V-c includes law enforcement officers certified under RSA 106-L.

condition_specific: acute stress disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' definition under § 281-A:2, V-c explicitly includes certified county corrections officers.

condition_specific: cancer including leukemia (type caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or known/suspected IARC carcinogen)
post_termination_formula: Eligible regardless of whether in active service, provided not more than 75 years of age or out of active service more than 20 years
filing_deadline_notes: Must not be more than 75 years of age AND must not have been out of active service for more than 20 years
notes: Prima facie presumption (rebuttable by preponderance of evidence). Applies to paid, part-paid, and volunteer firefighters. 7 years of service required. Cancer type must be causable by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known/suspected IARC carcinogen. Employer may require firefighter to undergo reasonable testing at employer's expense; failure to require such testing does not adversely affect presumption. Firefighters with less than 7 years who are exposed to known carcinogens/cancer-causing radiation are covered under a separate provision (section 6 of the act, referenced but not fully quoted).

condition_specific: injury, illness, or death from exposure to known carcinogen, cancer-causing radiation, or radioactive substances (including reproductive organ damage)
filing_deadline_notes: Worker must demonstrate injury/illness/death manifested during employment as public safety worker
notes: Applies to 'public safety worker' including firefighters. Worker must demonstrate: (1) exposure to known carcinogen/cancer-causing radiation/radioactive substance due to fire, explosion, spill, or other means during employment, AND (2) injury/illness/death manifested during employment. Rebuttable by preponderance that exposure not linked to injury. Employer must maintain deployment records for sites where known carcinogens were present under hazmat right-to-know requirements.

condition_specific: cancer including leukemia (type caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or known/suspected IARC carcinogen)
post_termination_formula: Eligible regardless of whether in active service, provided not more than 75 years of age or out of active service more than 20 years
filing_deadline_notes: Must not be more than 75 years of age AND must not have been out of active service for more than 20 years
notes: § 34:15-31.8 explicitly covers paid, part-paid, AND volunteer fire department members. Same 7-year service requirement and conditions as paid firefighter cancer row.

condition_specific: injury, illness, or death from exposure to known carcinogen, cancer-causing radiation, or radioactive substances (including reproductive organ damage)
filing_deadline_notes: Worker must demonstrate injury/illness/death manifested during employment as public safety worker
notes: Applies to 'public safety worker' including police (paid, part-paid, volunteer), State Police, and corrections. Same exposure and manifestation requirements as firefighter cancer exposure row.

condition_specific: cardiovascular or cerebrovascular injury or death occurring during emergency response
filing_deadline_notes: Injury or death must occur while responding to a law enforcement, public safety, or medical emergency under orders from competent authority
notes: Rebuttable presumption. Applies to permanent or temporary members of paid or part-paid fire departments. Emergency defined broadly: any circumstances requiring immediate action to prevent loss of life, property destruction, or criminal violations — including fire suppression, firemanic drills, criminal apprehension, and medical/rescue service. Presumption is event-specific (during emergency response), not a general occupational disease presumption.

condition_specific: cardiovascular or cerebrovascular injury or death occurring during emergency response
filing_deadline_notes: Injury or death must occur while responding to a law enforcement, public safety, or medical emergency under orders from competent authority
notes: Rebuttable presumption. Explicitly covers members of volunteer fire companies. Same emergency-response-specific scope as paid firefighter row.

condition_specific: cardiovascular or cerebrovascular injury or death occurring during emergency response
filing_deadline_notes: Injury or death must occur while responding to a law enforcement, public safety, or medical emergency under orders from competent authority
notes: Rebuttable presumption. Applies to members of volunteer first aid or rescue squads.

condition_specific: cardiovascular or cerebrovascular injury or death occurring during emergency response
filing_deadline_notes: Injury or death must occur while responding to a law enforcement, public safety, or medical emergency under orders from competent authority
notes: Rebuttable presumption. Applies to permanent or temporary members of paid or part-paid police departments, as well as special, reserve, or auxiliary police doing volunteer duty.

condition_specific: disease of the respiratory system
filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or first manifest during a period while member is an active member; first manifestation date runs from date of first physician notice or date of death
notes: Presumption of occupational disease (rebuttable by satisfactory proof). Originally applied to volunteer fire department members per statutory text (§ 34:15-43.2), but NJ Supreme Court in Lindquist v. City of Jersey City Fire Department, 175 N.J. 244 (2003), extended the presumption to paid firefighters as well. Requirements: (1) disease develops/first manifests while member is active, (2) member had pre-entry medical exam showing no such disease (or passed exam within 180 days of statute's effective date for prior members). Disease must manifest within 90 days from the event medically determined to be the cause.

condition_specific: disease of the respiratory system
filing_deadline_notes: Disease must develop or first manifest while member is an active member; first manifestation runs from date of first physician notice or date of death
notes: Statutory text of § 34:15-43.2 explicitly applies to volunteer fire department members. Pre-entry medical exam required showing no respiratory disease (or exam within 180 days of statute effective date for prior members). Disease must manifest within 90 days from causative event.

condition_specific: injury, illness, or death caused by smallpox vaccine or other bioterrorism/epidemic inoculation program
notes: Presumption covers 'public safety worker' which includes firefighters (paid, part-paid, volunteer). Applies to injury/illness/death from vaccines (including smallpox) administered as part of a bioterrorism-preparedness or epidemic inoculation program, or transmission from another inoculated employee/public member. Rebuttable by preponderance showing administration not linked to injury. Statute does not authorize mandatory inoculation. Included under 'other' due to vaccine/biological warfare context.

condition_specific: injury, illness, or death caused by smallpox vaccine or other bioterrorism/epidemic inoculation program
notes: Applies to 'public safety worker' which includes basic or advanced medical technicians of a first aid or rescue squad, and nurses/EMTs responding to a catastrophic incident (volunteer or employed). Same inoculation program scope.

condition_specific: injury, illness, or death caused by smallpox vaccine or other bioterrorism/epidemic inoculation program
notes: Applies to 'public safety worker' which includes paid, partially-paid, and volunteer police departments including State Police, and correctional facility officers. Same inoculation program scope as firefighter row.

condition_specific: brain cancer, bladder cancer, kidney cancer, colorectal cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, leukemia, ureter cancer, testicular cancer, breast cancer, esophageal cancer, multiple myeloma
notes: Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. Each specific cancer type has a different minimum years-of-service threshold: brain cancer 10 yrs, bladder cancer 12 yrs, kidney cancer 15 yrs, colorectal cancer 10 yrs, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma 15 yrs, leukemia 5 yrs, ureter cancer 12 yrs, testicular cancer 5 yrs (if diagnosed before age 40 with no evidence of anabolic steroid/HGH use), breast cancer 5 yrs (if diagnosed before age 40 without BRCA1/BRCA2 predisposition), esophageal cancer 10 yrs, multiple myeloma 15 yrs. Condition must not have been revealed during pre-employment or subsequent OSHA medical screening exam. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence that firefighter engaged in conduct/activities outside employment that posed significant risk of contracting the condition. Medical treatment provided as job-related unless/until court determines presumption does not apply; if court rejects presumption, employer's WC carrier reimbursed by firefighter's health plan.

condition_specific: heart injury or stroke suffered within 24 hours of specified firefighting or emergency activities
filing_deadline_notes: Heart injury or stroke must occur within 24 hours of triggering activity
notes: Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. Event-specific presumption: heart injury or stroke must occur within 24 hours of: fighting a fire, responding to an alarm, returning from an alarm call, engaging in supervised physical training, or responding to/performing in a non-fire emergency. Presumption does NOT apply if employer lacks a current physical training program AND firefighter lacks a current OSHA medical screening allowing participation in that program. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: hepatitis, tuberculosis, diphtheria, meningococcal disease, methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA)
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must appear and be diagnosed after entry into employment
notes: Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. No minimum years-of-service threshold stated for infectious diseases — condition must appear and be diagnosed after entry into employment. Condition must not have been revealed during pre-employment or subsequent OSHA medical screening. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence showing firefighter's outside conduct posed significant risk.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) resulting in physical impairment, primary or secondary mental impairment, or death
notes: Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. PTSD must be diagnosed by a physician or psychologist and must result in physical impairment, primary or secondary mental impairment, or death. No minimum years-of-service threshold. Condition must not have been revealed during pre-employment or subsequent OSHA medical screening. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Bladder, brain, breast, colon, esophageal, Hodgkin's lymphoma, kidney, leukemia, liver, lung, lymphatic/haematopoietic, basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, malignant melanoma, multiple myeloma, nasopharyngeal/laryngeal/pharyngeal, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, ovarian, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, soft tissue sarcoma, stomach, testicular, thyroid, urinary tract/ureteral, uterine
post_termination_formula: For retirements before 7/1/2019: 3 months per year of service, max 60 months. For retirements on/after 7/1/2019: if <20 years service, period equal to years worked; if >=20 years, lifetime coverage. Presumption applies if diagnosed during employment unconditionally.
notes: Exposure to known or reasonably anticipated carcinogen must also be demonstrated. Subsection 2 provides a non-exclusive list of carcinogen-cancer associations. For post-retirement claims under sub. 7 (retirements on/after 7/1/2019): compensation limited to medical benefits only, no wage-loss benefits. Pre-employment exam not required per statute text for cancer (distinguished from lung/heart).

condition_specific: Bladder, brain, breast, colon, esophageal, Hodgkin's lymphoma, kidney, leukemia, liver, lung, lymphatic/haematopoietic, basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, malignant melanoma, multiple myeloma, nasopharyngeal/laryngeal/pharyngeal, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, ovarian, pancreatic, prostate, rectal, soft tissue sarcoma, stomach, testicular, thyroid, urinary tract/ureteral, uterine
post_termination_formula: 3 months per year of service, max 60 months (volunteer firefighters always governed by pre-7/1/2019 formula regardless of retirement date)
notes: Volunteer firefighters must be entitled to WC benefits under NRS 616A.145. Post-termination formula for volunteers is always the pre-7/1/2019 formula (3 months/year, max 60 months). Exposure to known carcinogen must also be demonstrated.

condition_specific: Diseases of the heart
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time continuous, uninterrupted and salaried firefighters with 5+ years before date of disablement. Annual physical exam including heart exam required. Failure to correct predisposing conditions when ordered may exclude employee. Claims may be reopened at any time during claimant's life for changed circumstances. NRS 616C.180 (mental injury/stress) does not apply to claimants under this section (subsection 7 of 616C.180).

condition_specific: Diseases of the heart caused by extreme overexertion in times of stress or danger
notes: Volunteer firefighters covered under subsection 2 (not the conclusive presumption in subsection 1). Requires causal relationship by competent evidence that disability/death arose from performance of volunteer duties. Age limit: must not have reached age 55 before disease onset. Physical exam required once every 3 years after initial examination (subsection 4). Volunteer must be entitled to WC benefits under NRS 616A.145.

condition_specific: Diseases of the heart
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time continuous, uninterrupted and salaried police officers with 5+ years before date of disablement. Annual physical exam including heart exam required.

condition_specific: Diseases of the lungs caused by exposure to heat, smoke, fumes, tear gas, or other noxious gases
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time salaried firefighters with 5+ years continuous uninterrupted service. 2-year minimum service threshold for non-conclusive compensability. Pre-employment exam required (periodic exams also required — every even year until 40, then annually). Failure to correct predisposing conditions when ordered may exclude the employee. Partial disability may elect permanent total disability benefit under NRS 616C.440.

condition_specific: Diseases of the lungs caused by exposure to heat, smoke, fumes, tear gas, or other noxious gases
notes: Volunteers are not required to take the thorough lung-function test component of the physical exam (subsection 3). Conclusive presumption under subsection 5 applies only to 'full-time continuous, uninterrupted and salaried' firefighters, so volunteers have the base compensability provision only (2-year threshold, causal relationship needed).

condition_specific: Diseases of the lungs caused by exposure to heat, smoke, fumes, tear gas, or other noxious gases
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time salaried police officers with 5+ years continuous uninterrupted service before date of disablement. Same physical exam requirements as firefighters apply.

condition_specific: Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, tuberculosis, HIV/AIDS
filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure to employer per employer's reporting requirements; test within 72 hours (must be negative), then retest after incubation period but no later than 12 months after exposure (must be positive)
notes: Coverage under 617.481 requires demonstrated exposure during course and scope of employment, reporting to employer, and a two-step testing protocol. Employee excluded if: refuses testing, is eligible for other WC benefits, or clear and convincing evidence shows disease did not arise from employment. 'Exposure' defined as introduction of blood/infectious materials via skin, eye, mucous membrane, or parenteral contact; includes airborne TB contact.

condition_specific: Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C
filing_deadline_notes: Post-employment coverage: diagnosis within 1 year after last day of employment. If diagnosed with hepatitis upon employment, covered only if a different strain is diagnosed during or within 1 year after last day.
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for salaried firefighters with 5+ continuous years before date of disability/death. Annual blood tests required (hepatitis C annually; hepatitis A and B annually unless vaccinated). Employer must provide and pay for vaccination for A and B. Persons diagnosed with hepatitis upon employment are excluded unless a different hepatitis strain is later diagnosed.

condition_specific: Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, tuberculosis, HIV/AIDS
filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure to employer; test within 72 hours (negative), retest after incubation period but no later than 12 months after exposure (positive)
notes: Same two-step exposure/testing protocol. 617.481 covers any employee in Nevada who contracts a listed contagious disease during course and scope of employment; EMTs/paramedics employed by public safety agencies are included.

condition_specific: Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C
filing_deadline_notes: Post-employment coverage: diagnosis within 1 year after last day of employment.
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for emergency medical attendants (licensed attendants or certified EMTs/advanced EMTs under NRS chapter 450B whose primary duties are emergency medical services) with 5+ continuous years. Same exam/vaccination requirements.

condition_specific: Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C, tuberculosis, HIV/AIDS
filing_deadline_notes: Employee must report exposure to employer; test within 72 hours (negative), retest after incubation period but no later than 12 months after exposure (positive)
notes: Same two-step exposure/testing protocol as firefighters. 617.481 covers any 'person employed in this State' who contracts a contagious disease during course and scope of employment; police officers are included by the general employment definition.

condition_specific: Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C
filing_deadline_notes: Post-employment coverage: diagnosis within 1 year after last day of employment. Different-strain exception applies.
notes: Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for police officers with 5+ continuous years before date of disability/death. Includes sheriffs, deputy sheriffs, metropolitan police officers, and city police. Same exam/vaccination requirements as firefighters.

condition_specific: Mental injury caused by extreme stress from directly witnessing death/grievous bodily harm, or responding to mass casualty incident
notes: Not a pure presumption — requires clear and convincing medical/psychiatric evidence that employee has a mental injury caused by extreme stress from directly witnessing: (1) death/aftermath of violent event (homicide, suicide, mass casualty), or (2) grievous bodily harm shocking the conscience. Also covers responding to a mass casualty incident for state/political subdivision employees. Does not apply to claimants under NRS 617.457 (heart disease). Employer must provide mental health educational training to first responders including volunteers. Salaried and volunteer firefighters, police officers, emergency dispatchers, and EMTs/paramedics employed by public safety agencies are covered as 'first responders.'

condition_specific: Mental injury caused by extreme stress from directly witnessing death/grievous bodily harm, or responding to mass casualty incident
notes: Definition of 'first responder' explicitly includes volunteer firefighters. Same evidentiary standards as salaried firefighters.

condition_specific: Mental injury caused by extreme stress from directly witnessing death/grievous bodily harm, or responding to mass casualty incident
notes: EMTs and paramedics employed by a public safety agency in Nevada are explicitly included in the 'first responder' definition under subsection 8(b)(4).

condition_specific: Mental injury caused by extreme stress from directly witnessing death/grievous bodily harm, or responding to mass casualty incident
notes: Police officers are explicitly included in the 'first responder' definition. Also covers emergency dispatchers/call takers employed by law enforcement or public safety agencies.

condition_specific: Melanoma; cancers of the lymphatic, digestive, hematological, urinary, neurological, breast, reproductive, endocrine/thyroid, and prostate systems
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-d
notes: Statute nominally expired July 1, 2005 but may be kept alive by NY RSS § 480, which extends all temporary retirement benefits expiring 1974–2011 (same mechanism as § 363-a cardiovascular). Active status requires independent legal verification — original IAFF source characterized this provision as historical and inactive. Pre-employment exam required. Covers paid firefighters only.

condition_specific: Diseases of the heart
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-a
notes: Statute nominally expired August 31, 1976 but kept alive per § 480. Member must have passed pre-entry physical that failed to reveal evidence of heart disease. Covers any member performing 'fire service' as defined in the retirement system (§ 302(11)(a,b,c,d,g,h)). Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: Diseases of the heart
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-a
notes: Statute nominally expired August 31, 1976 but kept alive per § 480. Applies to 'recently employed' policeman who sustains disability while employed. Member must have passed pre-entry physical showing no heart disease. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: Diseases of the lung
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-f
notes: Statute nominally expired July 1, 2008 but may be kept alive by NY RSS § 480, which extends all temporary retirement benefits expiring 1974–2011 (same mechanism as § 363-a cardiovascular). Active status requires independent legal verification — original IAFF source characterized this provision as historical and inactive. Pre-employment or subsequent physical required showing no evidence of lung condition. Applies to uniformed members of paid fire departments.

condition_specific: HIV, tuberculosis, hepatitis
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-dd
notes: Covers any police officer or firefighter covered by § 363 who contracts HIV, TB, or hepatitis after contact with members of the public where there may have been bodily fluid exposure. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: HIV, tuberculosis, hepatitis
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-dd
notes: Same provision as firefighters under § 363-dd. Covers police officers under § 363 who contract HIV, TB, or hepatitis after public contact with possible bodily fluid exposure. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: Parkinson's Disease
statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-ff
notes: Covers paid firefighters. Pre-employment exam required showing no evidence of Parkinson's Disease. Disability or death covered. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: cancer caused by exposure to IARC Group 1 or 2A carcinogens
filing_deadline_notes: Presumption does not apply if more than 20 years since last assigned to hazardous duty
notes: Rebuttable if: (1) outside-duty tobacco/high-risk exposure was probably a significant factor; (2) no IARC 1/2A carcinogen exposure shown; (3) cancer preexisted employment; (4) firefighter is age 70 or older. Applies to hazardous duty firefighters only. 'Hazardous duty' defined per 5 C.F.R. 550.902.

condition_specific: cardiovascular disease caused by cumulative exposure to heat, smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes and other toxic substances
filing_deadline_notes: Compensation payable only if disability/death results within 8 years after last injurious exposure (8-year limit does not apply to exposures occurring after January 1, 1976)
notes: Covers paid and volunteer firefighters. Applies to exposures after January 1, 1967. Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment. Requires referral to qualified medical specialist before award. Compensation only for temp total, perm total, or death.

condition_specific: cardiovascular disease caused by cumulative exposure to heat, smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes and other toxic substances
filing_deadline_notes: Compensation payable only if disability/death results within 8 years after last injurious exposure (8-year limit does not apply to exposures occurring after January 1, 1976)
notes: Covers paid and volunteer police officers (municipal, township, county). Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment after January 1, 1967.

condition_specific: pulmonary or respiratory disease caused by cumulative exposure to heat, smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes and other toxic substances
filing_deadline_notes: Compensation payable only if disability/death results within 8 years after last injurious exposure (8-year limit does not apply to exposures occurring after January 1, 1976)
notes: Covers paid and volunteer firefighters. Applies to exposures after January 1, 1967. Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment. Requires referral to qualified medical specialist before award. Compensation only for temp total, perm total, or death.

condition_specific: pulmonary or respiratory disease caused by cumulative exposure to heat, smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes and other toxic substances
filing_deadline_notes: Compensation payable only if disability/death results within 8 years after last injurious exposure (8-year limit does not apply to exposures occurring after January 1, 1976)
notes: Covers paid and volunteer police officers (municipal, township, county). Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment after January 1, 1967.

condition_specific: cancer (not revealed by pre-employment physical)
notes: Presumption is for disability pension purposes. Applies to municipal fire department members. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: heart disease (heart disease not revealed by pre-employment physical)
notes: Presumption is for disability pension purposes (pension system statute). Applies to municipal fire department members. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence. Note: § 85A-14 (workers' comp system) requires cardiovascular events to be the major cause from extraordinary/unusual work exertion — not a presumption, effectively adverse to claimant.

condition_specific: injury to the respiratory system (not revealed by pre-employment physical)
notes: Presumption is for disability pension purposes. Applies to municipal fire department members. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: infectious disease: hepatitis, HIV, meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Infectious disease provisions effective November 10, 1999. Defined: hepatitis, HIV, meningitis, tuberculosis. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

condition_specific: brain cancer, colon cancer, stomach cancer, testicular cancer, prostate cancer, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, cancer of the throat or mouth, rectal cancer, breast cancer, leukemia, bladder cancer, gynecologic cancer (uterus, fallopian tubes, ovaries, cervix, vagina, vulva)
filing_deadline_notes: Presumption does not apply to claims filed more than 84 months (7 years) after termination of nonvolunteer firefighter employment
notes: Applies only to NONVOLUNTEER firefighters (paid at or above state minimum wage). Expressly does NOT apply to volunteer firefighters. Diagnosis must be first by physician after July 1, 2009. Prostate cancer presumption does not apply if first diagnosed after age 55 (claimant may still prove without presumption). Tobacco rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence that tobacco was major contributing cause. Denial must be based on clear and convincing evidence condition was not caused or contributed to in material part by employment.

condition_specific: hypertension or cardiovascular-renal disease
notes: Applies to firefighters of any political division with 5+ years employment. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition. Denial must be based on clear and convincing medical evidence that cause is unrelated to employment. Amended by 2021 Act.

condition_specific: disease of the lungs or respiratory tract
notes: Applies to firefighters of any political division with 5+ years employment. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition. Denial must be based on clear and convincing medical evidence that cause is unrelated to employment. Amended by 2021 Act, applies to claims filed on or after effective date.

condition_specific: cancer caused by exposure to IARC Group 1 carcinogens
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108(r); Article III § 301(f)
filing_deadline_notes: Claims may be made within 600 weeks (approx. 11.5 years) after last date of employment in exposed occupation; presumption itself only applies within first 300 weeks (approx. 5.75 years)
notes: Requires 4+ years of continuous firefighting duties AND direct exposure to IARC Group 1 carcinogen AND pre-employment (or prior) physical showing no cancer. Volunteer fire company members covered but must document direct carcinogen exposure via Pennsylvania Fire Information Reporting System. Rebuttable by substantial competent evidence that cancer not caused by firefighting.

condition_specific: cancer caused by exposure to IARC Group 1 carcinogens
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108(r); Article III § 301(f)
filing_deadline_notes: Claims within 600 weeks of last exposed employment; presumption only applies within first 300 weeks
notes: Volunteer fire company members explicitly covered. Must document direct IARC Group 1 carcinogen exposure via Pennsylvania Fire Information Reporting System reports. Otherwise same requirements as career firefighters.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers professional and volunteer firefighters. Employer may rebut by establishing an employment screening program per Dept. guidelines and showing via testing that employee incurred Hep C prior to job-related exposure. Enacted December 20, 2001.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Expressly covers volunteer fire fighters and volunteer ambulance corps personnel and volunteer rescue/lifesaving squad personnel. Employer may rebut via employment screening program showing pre-job exposure.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers emergency medical services personnel and paramedics (including volunteer ambulance corps and rescue/lifesaving squad personnel). Employer may rebut via employment screening program.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers Pennsylvania State Police officers and police officers requiring certification under 53 Pa.C.S. Chapter 21. Employer may rebut via employment screening program.

condition_specific: Hepatitis C
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers Commonwealth and county correctional employees, and forensic security employees of the Dept. of Public Welfare, with duties including care, custody and control of inmates involving exposure to Hepatitis C. Employer may rebut via employment screening program.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSI)
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed within 3 years of diagnosis; injury must not have occurred more than 5 years before effective date (October 29, 2025)
notes: Effective October 29, 2025. 'First responder' includes active volunteer or employed fire company member responding to emergency calls. PTSI must result from a 'qualifying traumatic event' (serious injury/death, child harm, life threat, mass casualties, crime scene). Must be diagnosed by licensed psychologist or psychiatrist. Benefits capped at 104 weeks. PTSI from disciplinary action or employment termination not compensable. Not required to be an 'abnormal working condition.'

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSI)
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed within 3 years of diagnosis; injury must not have occurred more than 5 years before effective date
notes: Active volunteers of qualifying fire companies explicitly included in 'first responder' definition. Same terms as career firefighters.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSI)
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed within 3 years of diagnosis; injury must not have occurred more than 5 years before effective date
notes: Active volunteer or employed EMS provider (designated as primary municipal provider or dispatched by PSAP/under mutual aid) included in 'first responder' definition. Benefits capped at 104 weeks.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSI)
statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be filed within 3 years of diagnosis; injury must not have occurred more than 5 years before effective date
notes: Covers Pennsylvania State Police officers and peace officers (18 Pa.C.S. § 501) who respond to emergency calls. Benefits capped at 104 weeks.

condition_specific: any type of cancer (occupational cancer)
notes: CONCLUSIVE (irrebuttable) presumption under § 45-19.1-4 — any type of cancer in a firefighter is conclusively presumed occupational. For firefighters hired AFTER July 22, 2020: presumption does not apply if (1) pre-employment physical revealed cancer, (2) fewer than 2 years of employment, or (3) regular/habitual tobacco use in 5 years prior to diagnosis. Applies retroactively to cancer claims pending on or after July 22, 2020. Covers active firefighters (during service) and retired municipal firefighters (§ 45-19.1-3 — covers retired members retroactively).

condition_specific: heart disease, stroke, or hypertension
notes: Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence. For firefighters hired AFTER July 1, 2023: presumption does not apply if (1) pre-employment physical revealed heart disease or hypertension, or (2) firefighter regularly/habitually used tobacco products during the 5 years prior to diagnosis. No such restrictions for firefighters hired before July 1, 2023.

condition_specific: smoke inhalation of the lungs or respiratory tract resulting in total disability or death
notes: Covers uniformed members of PAID fire departments only. City or town may adopt this presumption by ordinance — not automatically statewide. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition.

condition_specific: infectious disease including HIV (HBV, HCV) and other transmissible diseases
notes: Infectious disease defined as any condition transmissible by association with the sick or their secretions/excretions, excluding common cold; explicitly includes HIV, HBV, HCV. Disease must develop or manifest as a result of exposure during period while fire fighter or police officer is in service of department. Covers paid and volunteer fire departments. Entitles to occupational disability benefits under ch. 19 of title 45.

condition_specific: infectious disease including HIV (HBV, HCV) and other transmissible diseases
notes: Statute explicitly covers both fire fighters AND police officers. Disease must manifest during period of active service. Same definition of infectious disease as firefighter row.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: PTSD must be diagnosed by licensed mental health professional with a master's degree. Must arise from exposure to potentially traumatic events in course of employment or off-duty emergency assistance within Rhode Island. Presumption is rebuttable by fair preponderance of evidence that PTSI/PTSD is not job-related. PTSD arising from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination or adverse job action not covered.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
notes: Explicitly covers police officers (including chiefs, municipal officers, deputy sheriffs, fugitive task force, capitol police, environmental police criminal investigators, airport police). Same terms as firefighters. PTSD from adverse job actions not covered.

condition_specific: malignant neoplasms of central/peripheral nervous system, oropharyngeal, respiratory tract, gastrointestinal tract, hepatobiliary, solid organ and endocrine, genitourinary and male reproductive, GYN, skin/soft tissue/breast, bone and blood
notes: This is a supplemental insurance benefit plan (not a WC presumption per se). Covers paid and unpaid (volunteer) firefighters. Requires 5 continuous years of service in a SC fire department and active service within 10 years of diagnosis. Benefits: up to $12,000/yr reimbursement for out-of-pocket expenses, $20,000 one-time initial-diagnosis benefit, $75,000 death benefit. Implementation contingent on funding. Effective July 1, 2021.

condition_specific: heart disease
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must have developed while actively engaged in fighting a fire or within 24 hours from the date of last service in the activity
notes: Firefighter must be a bona fide member of a municipal, county, state, port authority, or fire control district fire department. Anyone joining after May 29, 1968 must have been under age 37 at entry and passed a physical exam showing no evidence of the condition. Condition must develop while actively fighting a fire or within 24 hours of last service.

condition_specific: cardiac-related incident (cardiac impairment or injury)
filing_deadline_notes: Impairment or injury must have developed while actively engaged in, or within 24 hours from the date of, a law enforcement incident involving unusual or extraordinary physical exertion
notes: Officer must be employed by a municipal, county, state, port authority, or other law enforcement agency. Must be under age 37 at entry and have passed a physical exam with risk factor assessment for coronary artery disease. If identified as high-risk but fails to undergo additional tests at own expense, loses entitlement to presumption. Cardiac incident must occur during or within 24 hours of a law enforcement incident involving unusual/extraordinary physical exertion.

condition_specific: respiratory disease
filing_deadline_notes: Condition must have developed while actively engaged in fighting a fire or within 24 hours from the date of last service in the activity
notes: Firefighter must be a bona fide member of a municipal, county, state, port authority, or fire control district fire department. Anyone joining after May 29, 1968 must have been under age 37 at entry and passed a physical exam showing no evidence of the condition. Condition must develop while actively fighting a fire or within 24 hours of last service.

condition_specific: cancer (any impairment of health caused by cancer)
notes: This section pertains solely to pension matters under Chapter 9-16 and does not restrict claimant's ability to file for benefits under chapter 62-8 or change the burden of proof established in § 62-8-12. Applies to officers or members of a fire department. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal any evidence of cancer.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
notes: Applies to officers or members of a fire department. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumed to have been suffered in line of duty unless contrary shown by competent evidence.

condition_specific: respiratory disease
notes: Applies to officers or members of a fire department. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumed to have been suffered in line of duty unless contrary shown by competent evidence.

condition_specific: Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma, colon cancer, skin cancer, leukemia, testicular cancer, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, breast cancer, pancreatic cancer
filing_deadline_notes: May use presumption for up to 5 years after most recent date of qualifying exposure
notes: Applies to full-time paid firefighters in fire departments recognized by state fire marshal's office and manned by full-time paid employees. Must have 5+ consecutive years of service. Must have been exposed to heat, smoke, fumes, or carcinogenic/poisonous/toxic/chemical substances while performing firefighter duties. Cancer screening exams required by condition type: NHL/colon/skin/multiple myeloma exam required after July 1 2019; leukemia/testicular exam required after July 1 2022; prostate/breast/pancreatic exam required after July 1 2025. Secondary employment or lifestyle habits may be considered. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence; oncologist (board certified) for death claims.

condition_specific: disease or cancer (broad — any disease or cancer)
notes: Applies only to counties with a metropolitan form of government with population 400,000+ (per 1980 or subsequent federal census) — effectively Nashville/Davidson County. Applies to full-time firefighters required to work in toxic environments. May be established by ordinance. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of cancer. Does not affect rights under § 7-51-201.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
notes: Applies to full-time employees of a regular fire department in a state, municipal corporation, or political subdivision that has established compensation beyond workers' compensation. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of the condition.

condition_specific: hypertension, heart disease
notes: Applies to EMTs and paramedics employed by a county with population over 400,000 (per 1980 or subsequent federal census) or a municipal corporation within such county, within the fire department, where compensation beyond WC has been established. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of condition.

condition_specific: disease of the lungs
notes: Applies to full-time employees of a regular fire department in a state, municipal corporation, or political subdivision that has established compensation beyond workers' compensation. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of the condition. Contrary shown by competent medical evidence.

condition_specific: HIV (human immunodeficiency virus), hepatitis C virus
filing_deadline_notes: Incident/accident report must be filed within 7 days of the exposure event. Post-termination coverage for 1 year from last actual date of service.
notes: Applies to full-time firefighters, paramedics, EMTs employed by state or political subdivision. Requires prior negative test for infectious disease before diagnosis. Worker must file a sworn declaration that they have not engaged in any high-risk behavior (outside-employment exposure, unsafe sexual practices, IV drug use, blood transfusion). Post-termination coverage: 1 year from last service date. Does not apply to death benefits under Tennessee consolidated retirement system or state death benefit. Pre-employment HIV testing required from July 1 2012; all infectious diseases from July 1 2015.

condition_specific: HIV (human immunodeficiency virus), hepatitis C virus
filing_deadline_notes: Incident/accident report must be filed within 7 days of the exposure event. Post-termination coverage for 1 year from last actual date of service.
notes: Applies to full-time paramedics and EMTs employed by state or political subdivision. Same terms as firefighter row under § 7-51-209. Requires prior negative test for infectious disease. Worker must file a sworn declaration excluding high-risk behaviors. Post-termination coverage: 1 year from last service date.

condition_specific: PTSD (post-traumatic stress disorder)
notes: Tennessee Public Safety Behavioral Health Act requires public safety employers (including those employing firefighters and EMTs) to provide at least 10 PTSD treatment sessions via health benefits, maintain lists of qualified mental health providers, and prohibit retaliation. This is a treatment access mandate, NOT a WC presumption. Does not apply to workers' compensation plans under title 50. Effective July 1, 2018.

condition_specific: PTSD (post-traumatic stress disorder)
notes: Same as firefighter row — Tennessee Public Safety Behavioral Health Act applies to 'public safety employees' defined as emergency medical workers and professional firefighters. Treatment access mandate only, not a WC presumption. Effective July 1, 2018.

condition_specific: stomach, colon, rectum, skin, prostate, testis, or brain cancer; non-Hodgkin's lymphoma; multiple myeloma; malignant melanoma; renal cell carcinoma
